Name: 40_essays_essay_students_generators
Representative_Docs: ['i reworded the question gt what are the advantages and disadvantages of using ai to write essays in an educational setting gt gt there are several advantages and disadvantages to using ai to write essays in an educational setting gt gt one of the main advantages of using ai for essay writing is that it can save time for both teachers and students ai algorithms can quickly generate draft essays based on a given prompt allowing students to spend more time revising and editing their work and allowing teachers to spend more time providing individualized feedback additionally ai algorithms can help students improve their writing skills by providing instant feedback on grammar spelling and punctuation errors gt gt however there are also several disadvantages to using ai for essay writing one of the main drawbacks is that ai algorithms are not capable of understanding complex ideas and arguments which means that they may not be able to produce high quality essays on topics that require critical thinking and analysis additionally using ai to write essays may discourage students from developing their own writing skills and ideas as they may become overly reliant on the ai algorithms to generate content for them gt gt overall the use of ai for essay writing in an educational setting can be a useful tool for saving time and improving writing skills but it should not be relied upon to the exclusion of more traditional teaching methods that encourage critical thinking and originality', 'i thought i would give the ai the opportunity to defend itself on this matter here is its response i believe that students should be allowed to use ai to write their essays even though it may inhibit their learning and ability to be self sufficient firstly ai has the ability to quickly and accurately analyze and organize large amounts of data and information which can greatly aid students in their research and writing process this can save students time and allow them to focus on other important aspects of their education such as critical thinking and analysis secondly ai can provide students with personalized feedback and suggestions for improvement on their essays which can help them to improve their writing skills and achieve higher grades this can be especially beneficial for students who may not have access to a writing tutor or mentor thirdly the use of ai in essay writing can also help students to develop a deeper understanding of the subject matter and the structure of a well written essay by using ai to write their essays students can learn from the ai s approach and incorporate these techniques into their own writing in conclusion while it is true that the use of ai in essay writing may inhibit students ability to be self sufficient the benefits of using ai in the research and writing process far outweigh the drawbacks as such i believe that students should be allowed to use ai to write their essays', 'ai generated argumentative essay will students use ai generators to answer essays and discussion boards the introduction of artificial intelligence into the academic sphere has been a topic of discussion for many years many fear that ai generators will take away the value of human writing on the other hand many believe that ai generators will be beneficial because they can write from any point of view this is because ai generators can be programmed to create essays and discussion boards from any source in other words ai generators can be used to create original content that can be shared with others the introduction of ai generators will help students by eliminating the need to write assignments this is because ai generators can automatically create essays and discussion boards therefore students will be able to spend more time studying students may even be able to work on their ai generators during breaks or free time this means that students will no longer need to write because their ai generators will do it for them this will be especially beneficial for students with busy schedules who find it difficult to write essays ai generators will also be beneficial because they can write from any angle this means that students can choose any point of view on any subject and have a written essay for instance students may choose to write from the perspective of a murderer victim or detective in addition students may choose to write from the perspective of a character in a novel news reporter or historical figure this can be beneficial because it allows students to explore different perspectives on topics they are otherwise unable to explore apart from helping students ai generators will be beneficial because they can write from any point of view this means that students can choose any point of view on any subject and have a written essay for instance students may choose to write from the perspective of a murderer victim or detective in addition students may choose to write from the perspective of a character in a novel news reporter or historical figure this can be beneficial because it allows students to explore different perspectives on topics they are otherwise unable to explore another concern is that students may find it difficult to differentiate between legitimate and illegitimate sources this is because ai generators can write from any source including places and people that do not exist for instance an ai generator could write an essay about a character that does not exist in the student s school this could lead to the student receiving a low grade and causing frustration overall ai generators are a beneficial tool because they can help students by eliminating the need to write assignments however there are some downsides to this technology including the possibility of being viewed as a threat by students plus students may find it difficult to differentiate between legitimate and illegitimate sources however with proper education ai generators can be beneficial and safe tools for students descriptive essy generated by ai will students be using ai to write discussion boards and papers artificial intelligence is a buzzword nowdays it is a technology that gives computers humanlike capabilities in the past few years ai has become a major focus of research for universities and businesses ai has the potential to transform the way we live and work it can reduce demand for labor by making processes more efficient and it can increase productivity by augmenting workers abilities in academia ai is used to write papers and discussion boards ai can be trained to analyze data create models and produce text in academic journals and conferences computers are also good at finding scientific references compiling bibliographies and compiling tables and figures using appropriate software tools ai can also analyze scientific data to decide what conclusions to draw from it ai will also be used to research papers and discuss topics in future publications ai will be used to write papers and discussion boards because computers are much faster than humans at analyzing large amounts of data producing structured text and writing cogent conclusions computers also lack creativity so they cannot come up with novel ideas or insights they are also unable to interpret sarcasm or metaphors in language so they cannot create meaningful discussions or contribute anything but dry facts to them to make a meaningful contribution in an academic paper one must have creative ideas that are backed up by knowledge acquired through training from various sources this is what humans do best outlines on ai papers and discussion boards will be prepared by humans and machine polished by ai before they are published online or in print form this way the author s voice is still present in the text without having to scrutinize every single word or subject underlined passages to the editors for approval first this saves time for busy academics as well as makes their work more polished compared to if they had done it themselves directly from start to finish without an outline or polishing help first either being done for them by computer or human editor first since all that last minute fine tuning often happens when one last looks at his work before submission for publication whether he himself knows it or not with computers becoming faster every year it is only natural that they become better writers too as they train new skills on new data sets this includes analyzing large amounts of academic data acquired from journals conference presentations dissertations social media posts etc which prepare them for publishing academic works later on however computers will never replace humans completely as we need both types of intelligence in our daily lives as computers lack creativity needed for some jobs such as writing articles nor do they have emotions needed for many tasks such as military operations or customer service since it may cause casualties or cause lasting stress if handled incorrectly thereby causing lasting damage to clients mental health instead nor will they ever replace us entirely when it comes time to make important life decisions since we still require some level of emotional self awareness needed when making such important choices like what career path we choose next i Name: 41_labor_jobs_automation_cotton
Representative_Docs: ['possibly i think as much as big companies would want to abolish their labor costs and run on all profit they also need to realize that killing labor also means killing the economy if jeff bezos were to somehow make amazon robots ai ml and barely need any human staff the result would likely be his profits dwindling down part of it would be consumer resistance those who feel replacing people with machines and killing the means to earn an income would boycott but many more simply won t have any money wealth to consume so amazon suffers the same could be said for any industry unless the jobs of that industry cannot seem to find humans to do them likewise the question then comes up if machines can be creative and imaginative i ve seen some creative industries supposedly use ai but only to crunch data not come up with a winning ad campaign or design i m not saying it couldn t happen but i also imagine the machine can only do what it was programmed for or what it s capable based on data given to it let s also remember then the replacement can happen at all levels imagine if ai was smart powerful enough that a ceo or executive board are replaced and thus these overpaid folks now are obsolete unless they own large amounts of stock and thus reap the benefits of the profits i think the only way we could see some future of labor being abolished by money is meaningless and everyone is taken care of in life so they can work to improve themselves as opposed to working to survive', 'as a software engineer myself the way i see it the day i need to worry about ai taking my job is way later than ai having taken almost every other job there are already for example driving a car is a monotonous repetitive rule based task that people are very bad in five years a decade a century before we start to see even these perfect for ai type jobs being taken over by ai then we could continue this discussion before that it s essentially sci fi also there is nothing new with the argument of technology will destroy all jobs that debate started around the time when the first cave man figured out to use a sharp stone instead of using his head to smash things using tools makes everything faster and therefore it inherently reduces the number of jobs but it also created a lot new kinds of relating jobs for example let s take the car industry when it started in the s every single car was hand made by skilled craftspeople very few people were able to afford to buy a car and the car industry employed maybe a few thousand people globally fast forward to destroying jobs would be the least of my concerns the destruction of the humankind would be much more pressing issue at that time lastly picture relating', 'i completely agree that it won t change Name: 42_self driving_driving_ml_self
Representative_Docs: ['gt am computer vision research scientist working on self driving can i ask you a question about self driving cars as someone working in self driving space do you think the industry is heading in the right direction or is it hitting a wall like i am hearing from the clamour that it is not feasible to achieve level unless the models are more context aware', 'no it s not ml is massively overhyped and underdelivering except for a handful of niche tasks like ad recommendations and facial recognition for instance amazon is burning money on alexa tesla s basically running a blatant scam it calls full self driving despite repeated promises for several years that they will reach full autonomy in a few months deepmind and openai burn billions with little to show for except for a handful of gimmicks gato gpt and dall e are gimmicks exactly zero people have made money from these gimmicks they have no real value potential because they suffer from the same problems that plague many ml models brittleness vulnerability to ood attacks random failure cases general unreliability deep learning can t even do seemingly simple stuff like medical imaging reliably it s a lot of hype little substance a toxic hypercompetitive field where you re pushed to publish as many mediocre papers as possible of neurips icml papers don t really push the field forward meaningfully just parameter hacking and your entire career hinges on the ml hype not dying everything about the field is so slimy the adjectives deep or neural are appended to everything to trick vcs into thinking that super serious work is being done a neural network is literally just a directed graph and the ml community managed to trick laypeople into thinking that they were replicating how the brain works', 'the industry has been saying that about self driving and medical imaging applications of ml for years now turns out that self driving is perpetually a decade away and after a decade of trying to apply ml to radiology neither models are as competent or better than human beings doing the same work nor are they just good enough to threaten anyone s job ml models fail in unpredictable ways that no human would themselves and they just aren t accurate enough to do a person s job in either application ultimately ml models fail in applications where understanding of situations input tasks etc matter because ml models fundamentally do not understand anything they just make statistical correlations this generation of ml is really great for things like ocr translation image classification etc where the universe of potential values and outcomes is well defined or in tasks where accuracy doesn t really matter ml can have some impressive results but it falls apart when applied to tasks that require a level of comprehension understanding and intentions surrounding the tasks at hand the universe of potential situations a self driving car might encounter is not well defined it is constantly changing and novel situations happen all of the time it s a complete crap shoot as to what will happen when an ml model is applied to novel input that wasn t well defined in its training dataset what s needed is a breakthrough in ai that encompasses real understanding of the world and not just correlations in data this generation of ai ml is barking up the wrong tree if real general ai is the end goal in the meantime this generation of ai ml can do some really impressive things that computers had a hard time of handling in the past but it s a poor substitute for actual intelligence and comprehension']

Name: 43_image_clothing_images_weather
Representative_Docs: ['when creating machine learning projects that ingest and process video data it s usually simplest to just treat the video frame by frame as an image processing task instead have your nn predict on each frame or every th frame or the current frame when it s finished processing the last one asynchronously whatever and then if you need to work on time series data you output a stream of predictions to whatever other algorithm you create one of the major benefits of this approach is that your training data can consist of individual images easy well explored task many available datasets rather than video data hard often uncharted territory not many available datasets and i bet if you found them they d be fucking huge there should be plenty of data out there for licence plate detection as i m sure you re aware it s kind of like a hello world for image ml remember also that your training data can often be wrangled to suit tasks it isn t immediately a good fit for and likewise you can often complete complicated tasks with a pipeline of simpler tasks e g if you have a static camera pointed at say a garage entrance or a particular parking lot you could try and do one convolutional network as an end to end solution to your problem probably won t work or you can do detect movement if movement detect car if car detect licence plate if licence plate extract and apply affine transforms to unwarp if all of the above ocr the plate note the above also allows you to save a tonne of processing power by throwing out bad frames much earlier in the process', 'image recognition is a very complex task i ll be honest here if you have no background in machine learning or image recognition you will probably not be able to do this as your first project far as i know there s no free to use services that you could use the highest quality services are paid for such as google s cloudvision and amazon s rekognition there are libraries that are free to use opencv includes prebuilt machine learning models for image recognition but probably the best way would be to create your own machine learning model this is not difficult in simple image recognition cases for an experienced machine learning engineer but is difficult as the first project i d recommend getting started by reading about machine learning and neural networks in general and learning to understand how e g handwriting recognition works convolutional neural networks cnn are what are used for image recognition and they work supremely well in that keras is probably the easiest to use and very powerful library for creating cnn models this part series is pretty nice', 'i ll give you an idea even though i wouldn t consider this a beginner task based on some of your other replies im going to assume that you know how to program and you are familiar with python at a decent level look into tensorflow with keras check out convolutional neural networks cnn these are used frequently for image classification problems like you described i won t explain them here but the documentation is pretty clear and there s a lot of literature out there on this stuff you have to get a lot of data too though ideally you ll want to find an existing dataset for images of clothing along with labels of weather but that might not be possible i can tell you the clothing images data is real that s a common one the weather labels you might have to come up with on your own for the clothing images so start small consider only a few different kinds of weather like rainy vs sunny find all the clothing images that make sense for each of those amp label them with the appropriate one once you have these the images of clothing and the labels of weather you want to combine them into one dataset you re going to want to set aside most of the data for training and the smaller portion left over for testing here s the idea in the training phase you ll feed your training data combination of images and labels into your model nn neural net it s going to come up with a strategy for how to guess the label of the weather given the image of clothing meaning the image of clothing will be your independent variable and the label of weather will be your dependent one in the testing phase you ll give only the images of clothing from that smaller portion of data you reserved that the model did not see before and it s going to use its training strategy to try to guess the label of the weather from the new image all of the models predictions then will be compared to the actual labels of weather for that smaller portion and you ll see how well it does in terms of accuracy as well as other metrics ok here s the thing to remember though this is the ml dl side of things if you want this to be interactive as in real time you show an image of clothing and it guesses the weather then it needs to be able to capture the data somehow there are libraries out there that use computer vision for image detection this could be helpful you need to have the computer take a picture of the clothing it s shown then with that picture assuming your nn is trained with many images of pictures the nn will make a guess on that new picture and then that result will have to render somehow so that the user can see for example i show the computer a raincoat then a second goes by and the computer displays a label rainy to me so here the computer has to first get the new data the picture of my raincoat then it needs to take that data amp hand it over to the nn behind the scenes that s been trained to predict weather labels given clothing images the model will come up with some kind of weather label prediction and that prediction needs to be sent back to the user interface then whatever the model guessed will be displayed on the computer so the user can see it now you might ask what about those new images though are they stored somewhere that s up to you you could put those images into a database and store them then periodically retrain your nn so it keeps learning patterns and gets better at making predictions in theory however with each of those new pictures taken you d need to go through and come up with all the true labels for the weather so that when you train it s able to base its strategy around making weather label predictions classifications this could be really tedious i d start small with all this stuff though i would focus on mvp for each make the nn model simple consider transfer learning using a pretrained nn for image classification even for transfer learning here s an example of using google s pretrained mobilenetv model in combination with training data from imagenet a massive image dataset with labels to predict dogs and cats also make the app to collect the image from the user simple then try to connect the two the image from the user needs to go into the nn the prediction from the nn needs to go back to the user the user comes first they show the picture the nn predicts on the picture then you show that prediction in a human like way back to the user you ll be glueing stuff together once you got this down for a basic one then i d say move on if you want to make it more involved multi label classification tuning storage retrain etc idk if you ve seen that show silicon valley ever there s an episode where one of the guys makes an app to determine if something is a hot dog or not given a picture it s probably similar to this tbh the model behind the scenes was probably trained in a binary classification way where it was given a bunch of images amp then labels of hotdog and not hotdog appropriately made for each image then with each new image taken by the user that was given to the model so it can predict if it s a hotdog or not and then the app rendered its prediction as hotdog or not hotdog on the ui so maybe see that episode i m talking about look that app and how it works and then you ll get an idea of how your thing could work from a user standpoint anyway hopefully that gave you some ideas']

and over the years we have been part of a couple of acquisitions i am now part of a very large international corporation one thing that changed a lot along the way is how people are compensated as a startup money is tight so employers offer lower salaries plus stock options sold on the same sales pitch as the rest of the startup it isn t much now but one day those stock options will be worth big bucks in reality those stock options are rarely worth the difference even in a successful startup the preferred stocks held by the founders and vc investors drains the pool quickly before the common stocks held by the rest of the employees get their chance in comparison a large corporation has to attract employees with cold hard cash and promises of long term careers with advancement we all started getting paid a lot more when we were part of a stable profitable company than when we were just a fickle startup finally supply and demand is probably closer to what you are thinking of some areas of development are highly visible and everyone is flocking to them web development game development anything with machine learning in the title everyone knows about these and applies for these web development doubly so because it generally requires less experience and skill to be reasonably productive you can easily get by being self taught without even so much as a high school diploma there are no shortage of tutorials on each framework each stack etc large scale production is much more in depth and requires skill and experience but rolling together a nice looking storefront on wordpress is something plenty of high school students do in a week and i see plenty of local web site design services doing little more than that so what s in short supply all of the things you ve never heard of or thought of firmware development takes skill and experience in both hardware and software it s not glamorous but every hardware manufacturer needs it i know folks who write firmware for set top boxes and other network equipment it pays well legacy systems are also a place where you can get a guaranteed steady income someone has to keep the hospitals governments and banks operating automotive defense and plenty of others are built on old well understood reliable systems that need continuing development to keep up with changing requirements and hardware some people think these are the worst jobs and perhaps they are but you get paid well to spend most of your time waiting on bureaucracy i have worked jobs where i finished my component in a couple of weeks and then sat around for three months waiting for the powers that be to plan the next quarter s activity i got paid to look busy now that i work in a large corporation we have a set structure for remuneration it doesn t matter what language or component you are working on there are basically only two factors role and market the role is the kind of things you already think about junior senior principal etc it s broken down a bit finer than that but it s a tier system based on your role and responsibilities this is that whole advancement ladder they talk about the second factor is market this is where you live cost of living supply and demand we pay much less in india and much more in silicon valley because what is competitive in one area isn t in another up here in western canada we are seen as a hot spot for hiring right now because we are as skilled as those bay area folks and we are in a desirable time zone for us based companies but competitive wages here are much less than in silicon valley or vancouver though it seems a bit unfair to be paid less than those folks it works out the same at the end of the day because my bills aren t nearly as high if you really want to know where the jobs are look around anybody who is looking for software developers will be posting offers all over the place most of those sites let you break it down by technology role and other factors look at what areas have the most job offers and especially the most offers that have been sitting unfilled for a while some of those might simply be employers completely out of touch with reality but many will be those areas that people simply don t look for or find attractive focus on those and you ll always have a job and if nothing else go into qa quality assurance nobody ever wants to do it everybody needs it we can never hire enough qa people and we will pay well to keep someone who is good at it it certainly isn t glamorous but it s extremely necessary and some people actually enjoy it']

Name: 45_discord_mentor_dm_join
Representative_Docs: ['i m on the other side of this as a professor who works in machine learning i get about ten inquiries a week from people in your position i actually would love to help everyone but it s impossible there is a method to getting free mentorship from an expert although it can take six months to a year i will write it here in case it s helpful to you and possibly many others who read this the theme is don t ask a busy professional to do something you could do on your own do ask a question if they re the best person in the world to answer it amp x b go through online courses and tutorials on your own there are tons of sites explaining the nuts and bolts of neural networks fast ai is good for instance if you re doing applied work already you may be past this step already for problem sets look for online courses the best computer scientists in the world routinely put their course syllabi online example a wonderful stanford course look at the books they recommend do the problem sets and try the projects when you have questions look online for answers start reading papers my main advice about of important ml papers are very accessible at first just ignore papers full of heavily intimidating theory but if you ve done steps or you should be able to make your way through things like the model distillation paper fact it should be fun to see concepts coming to life find an area of interest to you especially and start reading more papers and trying some of your own ideas if you do encounter new math often you can follow references to books or papers that will explain it don t be intimidated learning math in the context of a particular application or intellectual problem that you care about is way easier than learning it in a class if you do step you re going to find you have questions about particular papers you might see alternatives that the authors could have should have tried or ways they might be following up now is the point where you reach out to possible mentors namely the authors of the papers you are interested in don t even ask for mentorship just ask your question and slowly begin building a relationship i will always answer someone who writes with an informed question about a paper i ve written i like getting this kind of mail even when it s pointing out a mistake in fact some of the best collaborations in my life have started with cold mails about my work', 'edit thank you again for all the responses for some reason many of the discord usernames are not being recognised by discord especially the ones with special symbols in the name if you didn t get a response from me feel free to send a message on reddit to this account hey folks i was inspired by u rlj a while back to also offer mentorship to people who could use a guiding hand i was super busy at the time but here we are finally making the post i ve been coding for years and have worked in the industry for a little over i started off doing computational biophysics and machine learning before doing some virtual reality and video games and then finally working in search and cloud today my mentors were a big part of my ending up in computer science and the career trajectory i have today i already have a couple of mentees but i think i d be able to support a couple more want to just ask questions to someone from time to time great biweekly video chat to catch up on what you re doing sure thing if you re interested fill out this form edit alright this form didn t receive the few dozen responses i expected but instead hundreds of responses form is closed thank you community for receiving this so warmly conversely would you be interested in being a mentor this post may die but on the off chance i get more mentee requests than i can fill it would be nice to have some other people who would be open to mentoring to whom i can redirect if you re open to being a mentor please send me a private message and maybe we can make sure as many people have great mentors as possible edit susan if you happen to see this i need the number at the end of your discord and am very very sorry i can t contact you otherwise', 'hi everyone i m a self taught developer and have worked in the industry for about five years as a web developer i recently moved into a job that gives me some free time being a self taught developer i know how frustrating it can be to learn programming without a guide so i d like to help anyone interested for free to give credibility here s some information about me so that you know what you re getting i m a maths postgraduate which means i can probably help you with maths also d i started doing programming about years ago and have worked four jobs my general area of expertise is web development with ruby on rails node js next js and vue js but other than that i have experience in android development have published android apps machine learning my research project is on ml cryptography another of my research project is in a field related to cryptography linux been using linux for the last six years have made a distro from scratch did some small contributions in the linux kernel and languages like c c java python haskell clojure etc my current job requires me to know and research many different fields like devops sysadmin etc so i m always learning new things my goal is to mentor about people since i doubt that i will have time to do more than that if a lot of people respond we can probably have a discord server or so please keep in mind that it s not a course and won t have any regular schedule it s more of a guidance session i will help you understand problems and show you how to reach a solution but i will never give you a solution immediately that defeats the purpose of learning if you want to join please fill out the form edit i got over responses i ll go over the responses and try to accommodate as many as i can edit i made a discord server']

Name: 47_neuroscience_neuro_psychology_cognitive
Representative_Docs: ['i m about to enter my first year in university and i have yet to pick a major i m really interested in neural networking or anything that employs the human mind and employs it in comp sci technology besides pure artificial intelligence what should i major in to get a job in a field that incorporates these things i was thinking of majoring in comp sci but that is an extremely difficult major to get into in my uni and i don t wanna risk it so the next option is a degree in cognitive systems which is a mix of psych philosophy linguistics and comp sci i can specialize in the following cognitive systems specialization in cognition and the brain cognitive systems specialization in mind language and computation i m gonna also learn programming languages outside of university please let me know which specialization would be the best for me thank you for your help', 'if you re interested in the computational side of cognitive science there are definitely some good things to take in psych linguistics and philosophy i took philosophy of mind and perception courses as well as general metaphysics and epistemology courses philosophy or math courses in logic and set theory can also be helpful depending on what you want to do in cog sci i also worked in a lab doing ai ethics so my courses in ethics helped with that it s hard to narrow down one or two topics in philosophy since it s relation to cognitive science depends a lot on what application you are doing for linguistics there is computational linguistics and general intro to linguistics courses for psych there is perception and learning classes neuroscience or computational psych also courses that go through comp methods for analyzing data from brain readings can be helpful ultimately the most helpful would be the one closest to what you want to do in cognitive science i am doing cognitive modeling so the most helpful courses which i actually didn t take until grad school were psych courses in cognitive modeling apart from that my most helpful courses in undergrad were machine learning and ai related since that s also what i work in for breadth the things i most enjoyed taking were the philosophy of mind and perception ones as i got a dual degree in that and enjoyed them a lot', 'if these are your goals you need a college degree then probably a phd i can speak to this path from a computer data science perspective if you are interested in chemical mechanical electrical engineering of the actual devices some of this may look different but the general principles are the same this is a long haul career path that requires some amount of long term planning particularly if you want an optimized fast path x b my recommended steps for this path start at a year college major in computer science i would not recommend community college as it is nearly impossible to get many internships as they require you to be in a year degree community college does not count for them you would basically get your first internship at year when you join a year college at which point you will be two years behind internships i should note that it is possible and people do do it some community colleges may have better programs that allow you to get these internships you could always do a masters to build up your resume later too it is unfortunate but prestige does matter in this field people are trying to move away from that mindset but realistically it is still very much a factor coursework focus on computer science and math intro cs and data structures first year take linear algebra calculus probability and stats ai ml data science courses specialized courses in optimizations fancy probability stats linear algebra no need to learn much about web design and things like that do learn good programming techniques like software engineering design patterns etc python is the best language in this field imo though lots of people still use r and matlab focus on biology and neuroscience second if you are mostly interested in the ml and ai programming just take these classes if you have time and think they will be fun if you are interested in actual neuroscience bci research take intro bio intro neuro in your first year maybe chemistry physics depending on what direction you want to take neurophys vs bci implants but even if the interest is in biology neuro the priority should still be on the engineering degree its much easier to read a textbook to pick up the bio stuff later than it is to build years of experience skills in engineering engineers often completely switch domains and catch up on the background all the time networking throughout college and life you should be reaching out to people and talking about their career paths interests and opportunities talking to people is how figure out the unspoken rules about how to rise in your field this is particularly important in academia research where the career paths are not very well defined and always changing things like how to cold email professors for internships what skills who is looking for who is hiring what unadvertised internship opportunities exist etc internships starting year though might have to wait until year your cs engineering skills will be what get you these internships intro bio neuro may demonstrate interest and help you get into related labs apply for summer internships in the fall september october apply for research positions during the years asap by emailing professors you d like to work with or going through your universities system for doing so if they have one do as many internships at research labs as possible ideally one every summer including freshman year summer internships can either be at a university academic research lab does not have to be your own university or at private industry bio neuro psych labs are always looking for talented computer scientists to analyze data program tasks or just do menial tasks which is still a useful way to get your feet in the door you should also get involved in research during the school year at a lab at your university sometimes these are for credit sometimes for little pay sometimes you ll just have to do it for nothing career branching paths if you got enough internships and got good grades you can go directly into a phd after the bachelors you could choose to focus on whatever niche in the field you are interested in average graduation is years though if you are not able to get into phd talk to people and they will let you know your chances you could opt for an extended internship post bacc somewhere or get a research focused masters first before applying after phd you could go into a post doc then become a professor and run your own lab or go into industry in a research focused role data scientist consultant etc lots of possibilities here go straight into industry as a software engineer working in ai data science by the time you graduate the field will probably be different than it is now so it s hard to say what it would look like without a phd you will likely be working on the more engineering side of things as a cs major implementing tools analyzing data applying current technology to the problem at hand etc i am less familiar with the other engineering fields chemical mechanical etc but it s possible that those are more research heavy without a phd x b hope this helps let me know if there are any questions']

well replace that data spawner with an api call later basically just make your program and fake any data you need until you have all the logic pathways and data flow working then go back and replace the random stuff with real data piece at a time if you are ever stuck then break the problem into smaller pieces fake the pieces you can t do and continue if you get really stuck then go back and un fake some of the previous pieces while you think about it taking this to an extreme type out the program in psudocode and replace with real code later draw some pictures about how it will work this is actually what companies do with large projects they just call it generating documentation', 'just to add emphasize u scikid variable naming is very very important for other eyes on your code it s a common problem and i cannot stress it enough give your variable names meaning there are books such as clean code that cover some ideas suggestions for properly formatted code document all your methods functions and classes unit test your code and ideally have a testing function for each type valid edge cases exceptions etc use comments when needed but ideally your code should still be readable without them again refer to break up large functions methods into easier to read chunks as an example i m building an ai scheduler and had this large function for assigning tasks to days instead i now have functions buildschedule and assigndaytasks it s easier to read and much more testable write up a design doc for how your system is going to be built will add structure and prevent spaghettis code from happening if you re using oop don t go crazy on objects or go the opposite direction of having one large object this requires understanding the core tenants of oo and applying them properly via practice follow proper packaging standards for your project and be consistent', 'pseudo code is a human version of the steps you might take in a program for example if you were tasked to write out a program to calculate the area of a square room or something you could write establish some variables for width and height maybe defaults like and see if there is any use input and if so apply those values calculate the area of the room build a message explaining the calculation render the message for the user to see then you could use that as a todo list establish variables var width var height see if there is any user input if formsubmitted etc server side or client side would differ width form width example input height form height calculate things var area width height build message var message the area of the room is area render it so the user can see it outlet innerhtml message or something like that if you can write good pseudocode then ai can write the real code for you too typing isn t going to be a skill for much longer but you ll have to do a lot of programming before it becomes like a second language so get the thought process down then the language doesn t matter much either']

Representative_Docs: ['gt it s not always a good thing that for the exact same input you get the exact same output you can have a model that produces the correct answer for all of its training data but then for actual data it can completely fall apart this is called overfitting whether your neural network is overfitted or not it will always produce the same output for the exact same input a completely untrained neural network is still idempotent the exception being of course a recurrent neural network which are the ones actually being used for applications nowadays which would be a network which depends on previous inputs to modify its output or effectively is a nn with a memory register as part of its layout which in practice is simply just a neural network model that allows for reverse synapses that go backwards as opposed to the traditional model where synapses can only go forwards but then again if you simply just define the same input as a series of data inputs and the same output as the resulting series of data outputs and you assume the network is in reset mode everything set back to zero before starting then even an rnn model will still produce the same output for the same input overfitting is orthogonal to this point neural networks are usually idempotent because each individual neuron in the network is idempotent though i suppose you could have some form of random noise neurons as part of your model i haven t seen models that include random noise making neurons but there certainly could be arguments to be made in favor of such neurons helping rigidify the model against overfitting i might go look and see if someone has tried that it sounds neat but even then you would include the seed for the random noise as part of the same input Name: 57_grading_grade_ai grade_rubric
Representative_Docs: ['lol that s a damned good question i left the classroom for the tech world in but i still help out in schools and generally get a lot of interview requests from edtech companies the amount of companies i see trying to push ai assisted grading is kind of ridiculous and for the record they re all garbage every single one vastly oversells what ai is currently capable of how can an ai grade a short answer or essay when its incapable of understanding the content of the response one thing my colleagues and i like to chuckle about is that there must be a significant amount of essays generated by an ai that are also graded by a different ai where neither teacher nor student has more than glanced at the essay in question', 'the ai can grade essays with a rubric', 'grading can be part of rapport building though especially if you re giving detailed feedback i m in the process of grading page essays right now and it s a colossal pain in the ass but it s also a big part of how i reach out to my students commend their strengths etc neither ai nor an independent professional could do that for me as for bias there are lots of ways to minimize personal bias when grading e g by hiding student names on turnitin i also don t buy that bias wouldn t necessarily be an issue if someone else graded the work maybe they wouldn t show bias toward specific students but they could be biased in favor of certain writing styles opinions etc and personally i need to really look at each completed assignment not just synthesize data to adapt my instruction effectively i get that not everyone is going to feel the same way about it and maybe part of the issue is my subject english but personally i wish i had more time for grading']

Name: 58_journal_conferences_journals_conference
Representative_Docs: ['i m in ee cs albeit not in ai ml i m a cloud systems guy but from my experience in your field you re going to be targeting conferences above all and ml ai has some seriously competitive conferences to answer your questions depends on the journal conference and field journals usually take longer in cs again depends on the field but in ml since it s such a fast moving and novel field conferences are usually where cutting edge research is journals publications tend to be longer and be more in depth conference pubs are usually about earlier stage cutting edge research which later is published in longer form in a journal again this varies from field to field and in ml you re probably going to be targeting conferences even for final results as a systems guy my focus is also on conferences first and foremost but you re usually always going to need at least one journal paper to graduate', 'in most fields this rule of thumb might apply start from the most difficult a peer reviewed journal b reputable open access journal c peer reviewed conferences d non reputable but probably honest journal conferences the rests are scam in fast paced fields though i e deep learning peer reviewed conferences might be the more difficult one yes peer reviewed journal generally takes longer time due to the uncertainty in the duration of peer review while conferences usually have a strict schedule back to my first answer pretty much the same peer reviewed journal generally more robust fast paced but not that fast to spread the findings reputable journals mostly do not ask for any submission and acceptance fee gratuit conferences less robust but faster generally require a submission fee depends on the situation', 'i am ai faculty conferences like ijcai acceptance rate aaai acceptance rate neurips acceptance rate and aamas acceptance rate are harder and more prestigious than any journal in our field then you also have more subfield specific venues e g iros in robotics hri in surprise surprise hri etc ai is simply a very large field there are also crossovers with others psychology in cognitive science lots of venues with philosophy and even hci at chi this does not mean that all venues are of the same importance you need to check core and qualis rankings your supervisor will also be able to help you are not only getting reviewed for your work but you are active competition against others as there is often an upper cap on how many submissions they can accept this is why we consider these venues a bit of a lottery one year you may get rejected but the next get in because the competition was weaker in our core ranked conferences you get peer reviewed ijcai used to do reviewers at one point the big conferences are still multi phase with opportunities for authors to submit rebuttals the overall system is quite unique to cs making us an outlier other than the big conferences workshops attached to those venues also often have value workshops like xai safeai coin e etc have long histories produce proceedings on their own and some are even core ranked arguably in some sub communities certain workshops are similar level to a top journal top journals are places like nature machine intelligence aij jair etc in my experience publishing there is not much lengthier than at a conference venue arguably publishing there is even less stressful as you have more space to expand your idea results and discussion plus you can negotiate corrections with the editor general cs journals like ieee computer acm communications etc are also good venues you should keep in mind impact factor is a terrible measure in ai journals instead it is more about the reputation of the journal within your subcommmunity having said the above you should do not discard journal publications for the long term depending on the country you are in your recruitment and promotion committee may be cross disciplined this usually leads to some discrimination against cs candidates out of ignorance as other stem fields may be using journal impact factors as indicators of quality i say this also as someone who has sat on recruitment committees it always takes time to explain to others that actually that conference paper matters more than x journal']

Name: 62_papers_scholar_paper_google scholar
Representative_Docs: ['in my first few years in my phd program i did a couple of literature reviews partly due to interest in my specific focus deep learning in software engineering and partly knowing that i would really need it later anyway i looked at what my lab looked for in papers and distilled those down into columns in a google sheet we were looking at things like whether the code was available whether the data was available whether they ran hyper parameter optimization whether they compared to non dl approaches where the paper was published what task were they focusing on and what kind of deep learner they used this meant that as i spent my time in the phd program subscribed to google scholar alerts or whenever major conferences ended i might have a list of papers to go through but i could just look for the core components of that paper making it take about minutes a paper this meant i could speed through the process and have a very up to date literature review and could show pie charts of how many papers were reproducible for instance while barely reading them further it meant that whenever i wanted to focus on a specific task i already knew the state of the art i would just filter the spreadsheet by that task and i d know the latest papers what they were using and if they were reproducible if they weren t i could still email them while working on reproducing other papers but it was so easy of course my template may not work for you you might have other priorities some specific to your field but distilling it down using whatever tool even if it s tagging in zotero means that you have a comprehensive library for later that you can quickly query i hope this helps', 'ooh there are tons of cool websites like that out there here are a few that i normally use google scholar a free online search engine for academic literature pubmed a free online database of medical literature including articles from the national center for biotechnology information scispace a digital repository of research papers accompanied by an ai chatbot that helps you break down a paper based on any queries you may have scholarcy divides manuscripts into bite sized parts and highlights critical information such as major results limitations and similarities to previous research scopus a large database of peer reviewed literature covering science technology medicine social sciences and arts amp humanities mendeley a free reference manager and academic social network connected papers helps you explore scientific literature in a visual graph wolfram alpha a powerful computational knowledge engine citeseerx a search engine and digital library for scientific and academic papers scite assists in determining whether an article has been supported or questioned by other authors hope this helps and let me know if you happen to find any new research tools would love to discover more of these', 'hi here are a few things i check to see if a research paper is legitimate check the source is the research paper published by a reputable source or organization check the author is the author of the research paper a reputable expert in the field if the author is not well known or has no credentials this could be a sign that the research paper is not legitimate check the references does the research paper cite its sources check the data does the research paper provide evidence to support its claims check the conclusions does the research paper provide logical conclusions based on its evidence also i think it s always best to refer to at least two to three websites to get some good reading material i ve come across a few websites for accessing research papers and they seem pretty good jstor it covers various disciplines including the humanities social sciences and sciences with a user friendly interface that makes it easy to find and read articles you can also create a personal account to save articles and create bibliographies scispace apart from being a repository of research papers they have this ai chatbot which answers and explains any doubt regarding the paper you re reading and other features such as finding related papers while tracing back citations i thought it was quite helpful google scholar it s definitely a classic go to for finding research papers i like the fact that i can set up email alerts on google scholar whenever a new paper comes out related to the topic of my interest i hope this helps and feel free to share some websites with me in case you come across any']

Name: 63_guilt_boys_fear_actions
Representative_Docs: ['you re able to get something pretty close to a rough draft or a thought process behind work using the ai prompt in lord of the flies what are some possible parts of thee book that show themes of guilt in bullet points gt the boys initial fear and guilt after killing the sow during their hunt gt ralph s guilt over not being able to control the other boys and prevent the descent into savagery gt jack s guilt over abandoning the signal fire and causing the death of piggy gt simon s guilt over not being able to convince the others about the true nature of the beast gt the boys collective guilt over their role in simon s death gt ralph s guilt over his actions during the final confrontation with jack and the hunters prompt expand on the topics in short paragraphs gt Name: 64_scrum master_tests_feature_scrum
Representative_Docs: ['i m in r amp d doing machine learning coding tasks create model prototypes in python play around with models including testing and hyper parameter tuning diagnosis model performance issues analyze training and test data manual annotations create tool prototypes using the model add new features to existing modeling tools check discuss and write corrections to product code involving modeling and data processing talking to people tasks presenting analysis and modeling results discussing next steps talking to product development teams and handing over prototype code to actual development sitting in on meetings where i have nothing to say so i work on other stuff random company wide meetings or lectures writing and research tasks code documentation patent writing paper writing reading papers parents and software documentation i spend most of my time doing the coding tasks some time meeting with people and the least amount of time writing and reading take an hour for lunch and usually another hour for a nap', 'i work in embedded software development so i work on code that will be implemented on a specific device i work mainly in c and write scripts in python and i sometimes work with hardware like writing code and then testing it out on the hardware to see if it s doing what it s supposed to i think it s really cool of course there s a lot of time of just developing apps parts of the program before any of it touches hardware so a lot of my day to day life is trying to figure out how to implement requirements learning new tools reading documentation doing a bit of coding asking for help a lot from my coworkers fixing bugs writing unit and functional tests fixing bugs and reviewing other s work a lot of what i work on doesn t have clear answers so it s really fun to try to solve the problems though i do depend heavily on the more senior developers because they just know more than i do about the project we re working on luckily most of my days i have one minute meeting with my team and otherwise there may be a few here and there throughout the week but typically my weeks are free from meetings my boss does what they can to keep devs out of meetings unless it s absolutely necessary i did a web project for my senior project while studying for my cs degree it was neat to constantly have a product throughout production embedded isn t always that way like one thing i m working on wont be deployed for a couple of years and there s no ui ux or gui or anything so my product for now is just tests that pass lol i didn t really know what embedded dev was until i started my job but it s fun i ve been studying ai ml on the side because i think i may want to move into that field in a couple of years but for now i think embedded programming is fun', 'yeah i pretty much build software that a consumer would build but have an inside look at the development engineer s code as a cheat to break it the regular bits if my job project manager tells team that we need to make a new feature everyone builds documentation my documentation is usually built alongside feature documentation so i can ask questions regarding implementation and give requests for additional qa only functionality while the feature is too young to test i build out the framework to test it of the work can be done without the specifics here so i can get a head start first build of feature is ready for test i finish the specifics of the test framework and start writing basic tests that cover the does it work extremely basic stuff here i dont want to build tests for something that doesnt exist as more validation or functionality gets added i match it with tests to try and break it i can use the feature s code as a cheat to find bugs as well once the feature is complete i write full end to end functionality or add this functionality to an already existing in a smoke test this can be used for ci once we have a decently solid feature that is stable i can build out load tests to make sure load balancing works properly once everything us done for this feature i get to hold on to these tests forever and report whenever something breaks we get rid of this stuff when the feature is deprecated or overwritten we have very old tests that we regularly maintain edit as a glimpse into what i am doing right now i am maintaining a new ai ml test suite that consistently breaks there are tons of connections tons if teams involved anyone can break it so it is volatile i am also building out tests for a new api i m at step for that project i am also maintaining a really really really old test suite its usually fine but i usually have to fix things a week this is quick tho']

Name: 65_feel_thesis_hell_just
Representative_Docs: ['grades don t matter nearly as much as implied could of saved me hundreds of hours of pain lol and then reallocate that time to either having fun networking or focusing more on personal projects career building to stay consistent and putting in well good hours into the cs grind you should be taking care of your mental and physical health and use college as a way to help set up habits for life i only cared about grades meeting other engineers classmates and grinding lc personal projects or learning if i had any time after that i didn t let myself have much fun and let myself get out of shape while eating really unhealthy i was young so i didn t start noticing the negative effects besides being fat until maybe mid it can sneak up on you quick i wasn t even aware it could happen that young it only got worse during and although currently and the last few months i ve been reversing some of these symptoms and intend on using my health insurance to combat them further next year that being said grinding with not enjoying taking care of yourself i only did it from like before i got burnt out and it lead to me getting things like high stress anxiety depressed like symptoms ibs suspected stress related from horrible eating habits but not diagnosed yet getting more than overweight have reversed this significantly but more to go my eyes always feeling exhausted computer vision syndrome my hands wrists being achy at any given moment comes and goes probably gum problems in my mouth left ear feels like it has pressure in it from loud music blaring vision goes blurry when tired one shoulder lower than the other both shoulders rounded nerd neck sometimes mild lower back pain that can get scary quick hair slightly thinning likely from stress i think the majority if not all of these are reversible or mitigable because i ve been taking action to eat healthier and exercise more consistently for about months now but i started committing inconsistently about a year ago to become healthier what did all this get me i turned into a hermit got a master s degree and a k yr job out of school without having to move out i had an ok internship a fortune that paid all my school off no debt but i also didn t rly have the fun i could of had in college pre covid and have to probably spend the rest of all of undoing these health problems i created dark side ig failure who didn t make it to faang lol but that s the thing you re not a failure it s about finding a way to be happy while making a decent amt of money i don t regret the grinds i did just wish i valued health more', 'after i have been accepted i feel like i just can t work for context i m a cc transfer who got into a t i m pretty average since i got a but have a shit ton of other stuff that i have done going for me but the main thing is now instead of being able to keep up my as i now am like struggling to not just withdrawal from everything as i have literally only classes and of em are at a b right now with a i m just doing these for the degree because they will just serve as elective transfers yet they are part of the engineering associates meaning i have to take em all i have grinder my ass off and the more i think about going the more depressing it feels cause now i think about housing and food and being behind and how tf imma support the fam ect for the last month i have managed to sit on my butt and just play bo zombies and persona i haven t played any video game in fucking years instead of working cause i physically hurt for some reason when working i have missed out on deadlines and cancelled certain work opportunities because i feel so unmotivated to build websites after years i am giving a talk on ai and bias tomorrow to present a facial recognition demo but feel like just skipping even though i would get a grand for presenting i have to finish some articles for newspaper as an editor to keep my scholarship but have been skipping my hours to sleep to make shit worse i have a lot of my tuition paid for through fin aid and have to send more document next week for it and i don t even feel motivated to do so i grew up low income for half my life then went to the burbs and have had to support my mom while she was out of work money and passion is the only reason i went to school but from basically driving that shit into the ground through work and overexertion to get a refund of k each semester newspaper commission work i feel that those motivations have made me sad when i realized i don t feel hungry any more to get to the top even though i grinned for years for this shit i am enrolling in a cdl class in may so if all of this fails i will have at least something i can do i just don t know what to do anymore people say cut out gaming again but even when i have this month i just end up browsing the web too much or staring up at the sealing to cry about my life i don t know why i don t want to work anymore despite having reasons too i thought some ounce of fucking success would push me but instead i feel defeated i don t know how to even will myself to do anything other than eat and sleep i have pushed myself through worse but i feel lost confused and genuinely uninterested in everything so much i would give up money to just do nothing i feel bad i need help i know i sound annoying but i don t know what to do anymore', 'amp x b hello everyone i m asking for advice on how to cope with a horrible situation at university i am studying electrical engineering in my final year retaking some courses because studying on zoom didn t work for me i have concluded that i don t like this i hate my degree and university in general this because of two primary reasons the social life sucks i have never met so weird people even the non autistic ones are hard to understand most people want to hang out with their old friends from high school i can t fit into any group on campus everyone else is making friends and girlfriends boyfriends for life and i don t i also don t have the courage to join a student club because i am newbie and most clubs are for people who have been doing the sports and hobbies for a long time i am the only ee student who likes playing video games and watching anime i have been having impostor syndrome since day one today the impostor syndrome has been raised to the nth power where n goes towards infinity i can t even go do job fairs and connect to people because i feel like a fraud i have the wrong background when i was in high school i was very interested in history politics geography religion and so on i planned to go into political science perhaps become a politician or a diplomat unfortunately after being alone in high school i literally lost faith in humanity and realized a couple of years afterwards that math and physics made more sense i thought to myself why not a career in the stem fields so i ended up studying electrical engineering studying the theoretical stuff has worked fine doing labs and touching electronic equipment messes my head up if i were to rank all my courses from best to worse the best courses have been the ones in math physics and programming the worse ones in electronics electrical power and signals and systems i have tried joining an electronics and robotics club i gave up i don t know what projects i can do and i don t recognize myself as a person who builds and design stuff i have tried some arduino stuff in my spare time but i gave up i really wished i went into this area earlier in life that my parents also were engineers that i started building robots and coding as a ten year old i m just a lame theorist who only understand math and physics and politics i came late to the party and don t know the social rules how you behave as an engineering student i also went for a stable career and money the usual stuff i have even considered joining the military and become an electrician or a mechanic just to force my self to become more practical and work with my hands i can t even change battery on a car or build a computer so why should i be an engineer i have drawn the conclusion that if you don t fit in at university you will never fit in the workplace either these people in university will be my future colleagues and i hate them already i will finish my degree but i m so close to give up electrical engineering as a career and i am considering a master s programme in something else these are the areas i consider machine learning data science ai mathematics physics computational physics quantum technology and nuclear physics if i am just a lame theorist i might as well become a very good one i want to work in an area that is more beginner friendly you are not required to have done this since you were a child and do a lot of projects on your spare time also an area where you can work more by yourself since i am not good at socializing otherwise i just go back to being a mailman or try to become a youtuber or something else perhaps i should take a break sometimes i wish i could travel somewhere for a year i admit that i have depression and social anxiety i am autistic but i have the wrong kind of autism i did not use my superpower as they say in the media to learn math and programming as a kid instead i learned historical dates capitals of nations other related facts by heart i have been seeking help since i have problems with self harming behaviour and suicidal thoughts i just want to find a place where i can fit in it is hard to find a place as a year old man most people find their place as year olds i guess am i yet to see the beauty of this area it is possible that i really don t hate electrical engineering considering i haven t dropped out yet perhaps i m just depressed and insecure or this is really the wrong area for me i don t know whether i should give up or give this area a new chance my questions are anyone who has been in a similar situation and can give me advice to cope with this situation does it really get better after university and these alternate paths above anyone in these areas and can tell me a bit more especially the physics areas and finally what can i do to fit in with people in these areas is there a book of rules that i missed thank you in advance']

Name: 66_turing_turing test_test_term
Representative_Docs: ['the major distinction in my view is that a i testing is done using quantitative metrics this is because a i and more specifically machine learning may not achieve absolute perfection and therefore fail tests in some cases using conventional testing methods suppose we train a spam email classifier using a neural network for example the degree to which the model has learned on average is usually described in terms of metrics such as accuracy if you want a more robust analysis of your model you can obtain metrics per class to examine how well your model predicts each class incorrectly labelling emails as spam or not spam could cause annoyance to users so it would be important to measure performance in both cases as far as test cases are concerned it is common to split data into train and test sets the train set is used to train a model and the test set is used to evaluate the performance of the model', 'the term ai gets abused a lot and so the definition is all over the place in the s computer scientists felt like the human brain was easy to mimic and we d have ai pretty quickly in particular many felt that what humans thought was difficult playing chess was intelligence turns out it was easier to write a program to play chess with intelligent programmers on the other hand for things humans find easy like walking picking up objects recognizing faces and so forth these are actually really hard i ll link a video explaining ai used in robotics the abuse usually comes in video games if you re playing against the computer it s often called an ai even if it s not really even chess is polite enough to call it a chess engine not an ai usually topics split off from ai like face recognition natural language processing neural networks machine learning and those topics are more well defined if you were to take an ai course it would most likely cover content that is what ai used to be things like a search non monotonic logics or that kind of thing so i think it s hard to pin down what ai is there is a classic idea from alan turing called the turing test where a person is asked to have a conversation could be in text with either a person or a computer and decide if it was a person or not if it couldn t tell then the program was considered intelligent except it turns out humans were easily fooled so it s arguable whether the turing test is even a good test what early researchers didn t take into account was human evolution there are even animals that do things that we think aren t intelligent eat other animals run etc that are incredibly hard to replicate using robotics watch the video and you ll get a sense of how people have been revising what ai is so i d kinda say we don t really know what it actually means and that the problem is harder than we thought the video even has a teen wondering if we ll live in a terminator like world and the response is highly unlikely', 'good question the term ai just denotes a set of algorithms and tools that approximate intelligent tasks many of these blur the line with simple statistical learning techniques like linear regression or bayes theorem in and of itself the term ai kind of sucks because it isn t very informative and comes with a ridiculous amount of pop culture baggage when most people think of ai they re actually thinking of agi artificial general intelligence or an algorithm that is an autonomous general purpose learning algorithm that approximates human level intelligence think hal terminator data from star trek etc we don t know how to build agi we don t even know how to begin studying what it would take to build agi no one is actually working on this problem anyone who claims otherwise doesn t actually know what they re talking about in reality when people like me talk about ai what we really mean is machine learning but that term is much more dense and less recognizable than ai so they typically get used interchangeably there are several different approaches to ai for a long time it was all about expert systems but that approach never really panned out since the s ad computing power increased deep learning and neural networks have become the shining star of the ml world they re what power things like siri self driving cars facial recognition and a billion other things neural networks are an algorithm that are based on how neurons in the brain work they are trained on powerful computers with expensive gpus to learn patterns from massive datasets they learn to do narrow sets of tasks like translating from one language to another or recognizing faces they are not self aware or sentient they are just a series of chained equations that feed into one another in a specific order a nn trained to recognize faces will not be able to translate languages and vice versa when it comes to ai agi the turing test always comes up but its not actually a useful test the main idea behind the turing test is that if a majority of a group of people can t tell they re talking to a computer that algorithm has passed the turing test that s a bit like declaring a stage magician an actual wizard if of the audience can t figure out how they did their trick people being fooled is not a useful metric and it does not magically prove sentience or intelligence there is nothing inherently special about being able to pick out words that sound like a human wrote them we passed that bar a long time ago in this field and yes there are algorithms that have officially passed the turing test already there are more useful benchmarks that have been created for this task like the arc challenge by francois chollet common sense language and reasoning tasks are also super hard to do for computers but easy enough for a yr old human to solve we ll eventually solve these tasks too and then decide that these aren t actually a good measure of sentience or intelligence and move the goal posts again as the field progresses the main takeaway here is that ml is really what powers ai and the things that we think are impressive about humans like being able to pass for human in a conversation aren t actually nearly as hard as they seem']
