Name: 69_aerospace_aero_robotics_engineer
Representative_Docs: ['i m an ee with the intent to get an aero job i m more interested in space science applications not aeronautics but nonetheless the answer is yes you don t need an aero degree to work on planes in fact i think studying ee and gaining experience specifically in ee for airplanes is an easier way to stand out than being an aero major i m from the us so that s what i can speak for i ve had nasa internships as an ee one at jpl and they love my skills and i honestly don t know how i d distinguish myself if i were an aero major but it s so easy to stand out as an ee who loves aero i can t speak for the opportunities in france idk does airbus do aero work you re interested in i just have no clue sorry but the industry itself definitely needs ees airplanes need reliable electronics to function no matter what county you are from to address your area of interest i think embedded is good but i personally would stay away from iot partially because i hate iot devices and partially because that industry has different priorities from what you are aiming for i think iot is more product focused imo and there s emphasis on minimizing power consumption and low cost Name: 74_projects_project_app_portfolio
Representative_Docs: ['you being is not an issue i started my bachelor studies in computer sciences when i was i got hired when i was and now i m a mid level software developer so it is totally doable about impressing the employer work on interesting projects you mentioned you are learning java it can be used almost anywhere with javafx you can create desktop applications with spring boot you can also create web applications java is used also in mobile app development and so on come up with challenging project ideas and implement them if you can talk about your projects explain why you did the things how you did answer to problem solving questions and show an interest towards recent software development trends then it is enough to impress the employer many people try to get hired with personal projects so you ll have an advantage over them when you have portfolio that you can present if you can t figure out project ideas then i will list some that i have done in java some are on advanced level but i will list them anyways to give you ideas anonymizing network similar to tor browser integrated service for one lab device capillary electrophoresis communication with the device desktop application rest api database ok with database you would need to learn some database system for example postgresql remote web server management web interface java applet for smartcard you can develop software for sim cards and smartcards with java chess game with ai a program that is using machine learning methods on emails and learning to differentiate between spam emails and regular emails', 'work on whatever interests you your projects should have a quality over quantity and it is not impossible to come up with new ideas otherwise there would be no open hackathons and no thesis projects think what can be improved or what is missing and work on that if you say that nothing can be improved and nothing is missing then you are living in a perfect world where nothing is lacking also it can be something that already exists in one or another form for example try to do an online live streaming platform where users can do live streams or make an chat app that is based on bluetooth or an online multiplayer game or some browser add on anything that interests you but do try to avoid tutorial projects because you will not learn from them and these do not qualify to be in your portfolio either as it is not based on your idea nor your implementation will tell some ideas about the projects that i was making so you can get some ideas from them modded my old router into an iot device similar functionality as arduino an art project with zooming in fractals made different gifs with it a remote web server administration interface can administrate my web server without logging into it via ssh or something a demo for a website that is displaying all suitable places for beehives based on satellite data and machine learning algorithms the satellite data is displaying different information about vegetation water bodies soil pollution weather etc and as bees require certain environment to live in then this satellite data is feed into machine learning model that will tell then if certain coordinates on map are suitable for bees or not and if they are suitable then it is plotted on google maps an integrated system for one chemistry lab device a serial communication desktop application rest api and a database setup it is for capillary electrophoresis device that can analyze different liquids and then tell if there is any substance dissolved in it for example if there are drugs in blood or if there are minerals in a tap water a browser add on for jira project management environment a proof of concept for utf encoding to increase its possible character set size from current one a communication protocol that acts as tcp but runs over udp anonymizing network something similar to tor browser a way to interpret weather data as geographic data and build maps based on it it is possible to differentiate between land masses and water bodies based on weather information', 'the quality of projects matters not the quantity so having portfolio filled with requires some knowledge in distributed systems a ranking table based on one online game s hiscore api intermediate level requires some knowledge in rest apis and also in spring boot java framework Name: 76_engineer_title_scientist_ml
Representative_Docs: ['there are no correct answers like machine learning scientists work with technology language x and software engineers don t and what about a machine learning engineer is that a machine learning scientist or a software engineer and what about a backend engineer versus a front end engineer they do completely different things but they re both software engineers it s a blurry spectrum the best way to understand is to identify various job postings that companies hire for then focus on the responsibilities examples machine learning scientists analyze data and build models that glean something useful from the data product managers interact with a lot of people customers engineers sales etc and help drive the vision of the product that the team builds a product manager could work with machine learning scientists they both have to interact with the same technologies and languages because it s one project but their responsibilities are different the product manager might need to understand enough technical details to determine if the customers needs are being met meanwhile the machine learning scientist might need to debug why the model isn t good or adjust the model when the customers needs evolve on the other hand the product manager has to meet with customers a lot and push back if the demands are unreasonable meanwhile the machine learning scientist doesn t have to deal with unreasonable customers you can watch videos or read articles about all the different specializations software engineer itself is already very broad consider web development frontend backend full stack mobile app development data engineering machine learning backend infrastructure game development security engineering site reliability engineering product management project management i ve written about all of the above job areas in chapter of my guide to the cs major link is in a pinned post on my profile plus there s plenty more i haven t covered when a particular role intrigues you consider looking for more online resources and trying out some projects in that area', 'i ve been noticing a lot of questions seem to be asking about being a ml engineer and a lot of them are kinda misguided and confusing the role for other roles this isn t necessarily their fault because a lot of companies misclassify people or make people wear a lot of hats also the lines in between roles can definitely get blurred sometimes here s my take as a ml engineer imo a true machine learning engineer is spending time doing at least one of the following and often a combination of the following application centric engineering for machine learning what most think of w software engineering but with a lot more emphasis on performance and potentially getting quantitative depending on the requirements of the role and product the objective is typically turning a ml model or models into a reusable and scalable product you can expect a shit ton of asynchronous calls message queue based architectures concurrency etc engineering for data pipelines and infra what most think of w data engineering that enables machine learning can get a lot more quantitative or performance focused than other data engineering platform infrastructure engineering ops that enables machine learning what most people think of with platform engineer roles devops roles roles that focus on this point specifically often are classified as mlops engineers probably lots of focus on developing and administrating kubernetes clusters sometimes even on a hardware level helm kustomize security scanning and investigations building integrating monitoring and observability tooling developing automated integrity checks and audits release engineering ci cd pipeline development to enable above or model development sometimes ml engineers get involved with the development of the statistical models themselves but this is a bit of job scope creep and getting into the territory of data scientists ml researchers etc i have only occasionally gotten involved with this my current role is mostly with a lot of and not much of in the past i worked in a role that had a lot of some of and a very little of grad degrees preferred not required phd is overkill unless you are aiming for a really niche research role', 'i think it s not unreasonable to make the shift to maybe a ml ops role with your background something more infrastructure rather than the granular ml roles either way you look at it you ll have a learning curve but i ll detail out some common roles to help you pinpoint where you want to land data scientist they are the individuals who actually build ml models and can find patterns in company data researches and experiments with data usually has higher education in a math heavy field builds ml models with a unique understanding of underlying functionality typically does not have a software background and reviewing their code will tell the same story machine learning engineer has intermediate to advance knowledge of ml algorithms and their use case typically comes from a software background main functionality is to take a model built by a data scientist and serve it up to an end user or in other words productionalize the model most companies allow ml engineers to also experiment and build their own models in their own line of work but it s not necessary for the job most use to be software engineers that shifted in the ml space ml ops engineer these individuals are usually the last piece to a ml lifecycle still a fairly new job but they monitor the ml models after they are set up in production they typically set up automation and alerts for data drift concept drift etc which can be detrimental to a company if the model is a big money maker this is essentially a devops engineer in the ml space same sort of role with all of that at most places there is overlap i ve seen an ml engineer do the work of a data scientist and a data scientist do the work of a ml engineer same goes with the ml ops process typically there is always overlap but if people are placed right i believe the above descriptions highlight the responsibilities well']

Name: 77_algebra_linear_linear algebra_calculus
Representative_Docs: ['if you can do algebra you can do linear algebra it s a branch off algebra it isn t part of the main trunk of math that takes you up to calculus schools don t teach it earlier than college because they assume you don t have the mental capacity to study more than one field of math and they want to focus on the main trunk there are several smaller offshoot branches of math such as statistics group theory chaos theory game theory discrete math etc you can learn them earlier but fewer people have learned them and if your teacher is more than years out of college he she may have forgotten it you re most likely going to need to study these fields independently using online sources the key part of linear algebra you ll need is matrix algebra if a single algebra equation is a point a d grid of points is a matrix and a d stack of matrices is a tensor ai ml are recursive looping tensors where a process flows through matrices and where they remember something like the way a for loop counter works only it s like having multiple counters work at the same time and they talk to each other linear algebra also focuses on vectors which is also relevant to ai ml you can start with ai ml without la but you ll have a stronger foundation if you do la first', 'linear algebra i is enough for any cs student la ii is only useful for mathematicians and physicists basically pay attention in your la class you will come to regret it if you don t it s generally popped up everywhere i go in my studies machine learning contains linear algebra computer graphics contains linear algebra image analysis and processing contains linear algebra robotics contains linear algebra but in general you ll come to realize that all math stuff is relevant not for programming but for computer science', 'linear algebra is going to be clutch for anything with ml ai']

t need hormones or surgery to consider yourself transgender but for a doctor treating you that data is very relevant my point is that for a hypothetical medical use case the treatments you have undergone are relevant information there are also arguments that your identity might be as well for example if you wish to ensure a doctor uses correct pronouns or otherwise treats you in line with your gender identity though see below for some of the trade offs this presents i am going to hold fast on collecting irrelevant information being unethical if an application has the capacity to differentiate behavior on any kind of status it is very likely it will be used to discriminate amazon s applicant screener ai used gender as a variable and immediately became sexist because the training data was biased any data that is collected is likely to get repackaged and sold to data brokers i sure as heck wouldn t want gender identity information sold to a third party where it could subsequently leak to extremist groups or oppressive governments there are so so many examples of demographic targeting going horribly wrong and the benefits are very limited slightly better ad targeting there are ways to make it less wrong if the data is anonymized while it still raises concerns about discrimination it at least mitigates the risk of demographic data being used to target someone directly if the data is never resold if the data is only used for research and not to discriminate application behavior having a girls mode or something similar for an app is obviously wrong imagine if amazon exclusively showed dresses to people identifying as women or lowes only showed power tools to those identifying as men these are extreme examples of course but they illustrate that there are serious issues with discriminating app behavior on gender lines but there are use cases like after the fact checks for discrimination or sociological research where that data can be used for good if the data is collected in a legal environment where it is guaranteed not to be used against the customer there are plenty of countries around the world where identifying as trans would open you up to criminal prosecution or worse even in nominally free countries like the us there are increasingly legal threats to trans folk a government can never get access to data you don t have the core principle of consumer privacy is only collect what you need for example an address could be used by a stalker or abuser to track you down if they got access to your account just use a zipcode and you get the same feature for cc billing purposes the fact that the industry has flagrantly violated this principle for decades doesn t mean it isn t a principle tl dr anonymized demographic data is sometimes ok but must be used with extreme care in particular feeding it to ml algorithms is very dangerous non anonymized demographic data is dangerous and should only be collected to power important application features i will also say while consensus is not good evidence of ethicality i contacted a few dozen developers and a data privacy consultant on this and the universal consensus was that collecting unrelated demographic data ranged from questionable to unacceptable most agreed it could be made ethical by following rules explicit opt in it has to be very clear you can not submit and no app features should be gated on using it a clear explicit minimal list of what it will be used for no open ended use cases like improved recommendations or product enhancements no legalise or read our terms of service a clear short explicit list of exactly what entities the data will be shared with again no legalise or long documents any change to the terms of use allow the user to delete their data in response all data can be deleted at any time upon request now a legal requirement in several jurisdictions but often violated the request should be effortless a single link click not a phone call and certainly not anything requiring sending a letter']

Name: 83_demand_programmers_datacenter_software
Representative_Docs: ['others have expressed this idea more eloquently than i can but fundamentally i think software is the closest thing we have to magic a capability might not exist right now but if you cast the right spell code you can just create new capabilities out of thin air by typing on your keyboard the appreciation for this tends to get lost among meetings and deadlines and other corporate drudgery but it s really quite amazing if you think about it i think that software is also much more accessible than other engineering fields because you can learn and get hands on experience with basically anything with just a laptop and internet connection you don t need any special equipment or fancy labs and manufacturing facilities excluding some computationally intensive fields like machine learning access to tooling is largely democratized with open source software and cloud services so even a solo dev can accomplish a ton only limited by time and creativity finally unlike fields like biology where there s a ton of stuff that we simply don t know you can learn how basically any part of the software stack works if you have the curiosity to dig into it there are no mysteries it s all man made some people may find this overwhelming but if you enjoy finding out how stuff works it s an awesome field to be in', 'considering most countries industry associations call for more and cheaper software engineers i don t think this is true the focus of the job will probably change over time but this is normal for every other job as well i know the digital world is a fast moving field but even still think about all the stuff that is out there and how much we struggle to move forward with legacy bagage we were and are still using combustion engines a technology that is over years old and mechanical engineers are not running out of jobs aaany time soon even if everything electrifies just think about the software that the us department of labour uses and the backend software of insurance companies the us dol was looking for cobol programmers when covid hit because of the money cheques they wanted to mail everyone surprise surprise some of their systems were so old that they ran on cobol programmed software in the year and they haven t fully transitioned out yet but nobody these days has the knowledge base to do cobol systems anymore you can t hit them with the ai buzzword and they will magically solve all of their problems with ai starting in some international payment settlement and clearance systems across the globe still run on mainframe systems and cobol software as well ai will not help solve any of their problems in the next years imagine how much maintanence legacy development and software systems there are out there that are not going to be helped out by replacing all their legacy maintainers with some strange concept of ai', 'i don t think so if that were the case then we wouldn t need so many programmers today seeing as how our tooling has improved so much since the s there is an economic phenomenon where demand increases as price decreases there is a further ecosystem effect in technology the greater the number of people that use a technology the greater the demand for integration with the technology as software capability gets cheaper more people will want that capability as more people use a software capability the more people will that will also want to use that capability for example datacenters are extremely expensive with the advent of cloud datacenter compute is now cheaper than ever it is easier to provision datacenter compute than ever before one would think that this would have saturated the datacenter demand on the contrary there is greater demand for datacenter compute than ever before furthermore there are more new customers for datacenter compute than ever before the same happened in the past for personal computing internet smartphones machine learning if general software engineering became cheap per code product then there would be greater demand for code the lessening of per product cost may not materialize into decreasing developer wages as it may be accompanied by expectations of greater developer productivity and increasing complexity of systems in fact this is what we have seen in the past two decades with the advent of various powerful frameworks and programming tools the question is when demand would be saturated but given the scale of legacy code and the difference between the current state of technology penetration and the idealized state of technology penetration which i m guessing from the number of startups that arise my guess is that we are far from this saturation even assuming expectations remain the same through time which is almost certainly not the case']

Name: 98_ll_college_computer science_school
Representative_Docs: ['so are you trying to do a bachelor s in ee or comp sci have you decided which one if ee then i would strongly recommend brushing up on math skills since you ll ideally be taking calculus starting in your first semester all of the engineering classes will rely heavily on math as a foundation khan academy is a great resource for all the math at a high school through early college level for calculus there is also professor leonard on youtube and paul s online math notes for comp sci math is not as important although you will still probably have to do some calculus for the degree some considerations for comp sci try to decide what area you want to specialize in at first data analysis machine learning web development mobile app development front end user interface cybersecurity what people refer to as programming is a wide range of skills and somebody who is great at one of the above may not be good at another having a more specific idea of what you want to do will give you direction and focus if you don t know just pick something for now and you can learn the other stuff later if you realize you like it more but beyond the very basic stuff that s in every beginners online tutorial it is hard to learn programming without some kind of focus once you have decided on something do a project ideally find a project that is easy to get a minimum viable product but that you can also extend as much as you want later start small then add features be willing to just jump in without knowing a lot i was very skeptical of this advice for a long time but it is true that you will learn more by doing things than by trying to get a perfect grasp on the fundamentals before considering yourself ready study things as they are needed general advice for college don t rush it i am a few years older than most of my peers so i understand feeling behind however your degree will not mean very much to employers if all you did was grind quickly through classes and have nothing on your resume leveraging the opportunity to get internships research experience project experience networking etc is a huge part of the value of a college education there is a social into computer science than it but in every field it ranges from a useful skill to an absolute necesity depending on the specialization it s also good to see if you actually would like something like necessity and as a college grad i can tell you that school sucks at teaching you how to program but it indispensable when it comes to theory even with classes for things like java or c you end up spending lots of your own time learning if you wanna get ahead take time to research the field you want to get into and don t rush there s no reason to at the end of the day it depends on what you want to go for some have different requirements and expectations of their applicants additionally each one might expect you to pick up other skills specific to that line of work before they even hire one though traditionally cs majors tend to go into these fields as well also if you want to do programming for scientific purposes or idk program things like rockets or pacemakers there may be an just giving you classes on how to make a website or only give you software development job ready skills you re also taking things like discrete math data structures and algorithms operating systems as well as classes that deal with internet protocol and human computer interaction computer science is a theoretical field it s not just coding though it almost always incorporates it in some way and if as software engineers data scientists data analysts etc gt how long did it take for you to find your first job i started programming in the summer of and got a full time time job working as a data Name: 100_resume_engineer_learning engineer_feedback

thanks', 'while people like to think of the interview process selecting the best candidate as defined as most accomplished or most capable its more along the lines of selecting for the least risky candidate in particular a candidate with a phd in bioinformatics and an emphasis on machine learning applying to a web development position is a very risky hire as they are likely to go look for something that more closely aligns with their skills sooner or is looking for a higher compensation based on their degree having papers and research on the resume for a regular old software development web development position again signals to the people doing the resume review that you are interested in research more than the nuts and bolts of software development it also suggests to a reviewer that you didn t read the position description putting research on a web dev position and are not interested in working there if this was for a research level data science position i m sure you are familiar with the phd level researchers being much more prone to a small world phenomena and you had better not be a jerk because someone worked with someone who worked with you additionally the applicability of your researcher plays a strong part in the is this a person who could do this job i recall seeing a job posted at mcdonalds for a ml researcher who had worked with bilingual natural language programming models and a plus being proficient in spanish someone with a background in image recognition would likely not be a good fit for that position', 'i can understand the Name: 101_generated_content_ai generated_text
Representative_Docs: ['i asked for citations of actual research papers and i got one research paper that explores these ideas in more detail is towards the detection of automated text generation using style transfer by liu et al this paper presents a method for detecting ai generated text using style transfer and evaluates its performance on a dataset of human written and ai generated text the results show that the method is able to detect ai generated text with high accuracy', 'i fed your question to chatgpt verbatim here s it s response there are a few ways that ai generated content can be detected one way is to use a technique called style transfer to compare the writing style of the content in question to a large sample of known human written content if the writing style is significantly different it is more likely to be ai generated additionally ai generated content often lacks the subtle nuances and context dependent meaning that is characteristic of human writing so this can also be a clue that the content was generated by an ai', 'ai generated response it sounds like you re concerned about posts on this forum that you believe may have been written by ai or bots while i m not able to access the forum you re talking about i can tell you that it s not uncommon for ai generated content to be difficult to distinguish from human generated content in fact some ai systems are specifically designed to generate content that is indistinguishable from content written by a human if you believe that there are posts on the forum that were written by ai or bots the best thing to do is to flag those posts for moderation most forums have a system in place for users to flag posts that violate the forum s rules or are otherwise inappropriate flagging a post will bring it to the attention of the moderators who can then take appropriate action in the meantime it s important to remember that not all posts written by ai or bots are necessarily bad while some may be spam or advertisements others may be legitimate attempts to share information or engage with the community as long as the posts comply with the forum s rules there s no reason to automatically dismiss them ultimately it s up to the moderators to decide whether a post should be removed or not']

it be psychological philosophical political etc something that invites the viewer to reflect on the human nature and introspect as such i m considering using my free electives to explore another subject that helps me to this end my interest is primarily in screenwriting or literature with the goal of being adapted in some form of cinema that brings it to life such as animation tv series film theater etc i have included some examples of the type of works that inspire me the most with brief explanations at the very end the subjects that i m considering as of right now include changing my philosophy minor to a major pros explores big and interesting questions spans many different fields can be extremely helpful in bringing up and addressing a piece s central themes can add a layer of abstraction and or make the viewer reflect on its themes since this would be a whole major i would be able to explore a lot more sophisticated concepts in a lot more depth an additional major that will be helpful across virtually any career i end up pursuing cons not directly applicable can become mundane at higher levels feels like a bit of a circle jerk amongst wealthy old heterosexual white men although that seems to be shifting in recent years a minor in cinema studies pros discusses different techniques in films film analysis various movements and theories and the philosophical and artistic aspects of films very interesting and applicable room to explore all aspects of cinema whether it be artistic creative theoretical or technical con might be redundant time might be better spent studying more abstract material that cannot be learned easily on my own a minor in art history pros explores development of artistic ideas over time teaches how to analyze artistic works explores alternative mediums of art a subject i m not familiar with and hence it might open new creative avenues cons its about analyzing works of art and not necessarily making them the two are distinct and the former may not translate into the latter analyzing too many alternative mediums of art may not be the most efficient use of my time a minor in creative writing pros the most obvious and direct choice for my interests teaches how to produce creative works has multiple classes specifically in creative screenwriting and filmmaking cons many of these classes can also be taken as a part of the cinema studies minor some classes can be boring and dull especially the lower level ones where you don t get as much creative freedom and feedback on your work a minor in psychology pros explores topics about human nature can offer insight into how we work and think can help in making more interesting and realistic characters with depth can appropriately depict psychological phenomenon and disorders if needed lots of interesting classes in different useful topics like social psych abnormal psych psych in films psych of hate amp prejudice developmental psych etc can depict character growth and development better psychology is always useful regardless of whether or not i end up pursuing these interests as a career cons psychology is a science not a creative subject that would help me nourish my creativity psychology classes are absolutely gargantuan at my university and a minor in english pros explores various literary theories and lenses for analyzing literary works will significantly improve my writing skills exposure to and study of many of the best works in literature cons not particularly interesting to me focuses on literature not creative writing or screenwriting already have decent writing skills and either way my writing skills can also be further developed by many others in the list there are also several other subjects that are both interesting and may be helpful albeit to a lesser degree and not directly including sociology political science history theater arts and women amp gender studies i m also open to any others i may have overlooked any thoughts on which may be the most helpful am i missing something i would appreciate any input p s i am also an actor or at least one in training and am currently taking classes in acting which cover topics like creating and depicting characters various acting techniques and theories characterization script analysis etc hence i have some knowledge of the other side of screenwriting scriptwriting too which brings a story to life i just wanted to mention that because i think its an invaluable skill to have when pursuing something in creative artwork writing here are some examples similar to the kind of screenwriting i m interested in these include works from studio ghibli like spirited away and howl s moving castle which have a magical element woven into their soft worldbuilding with core themes that manifest themselves throughout the films such as coming of age childhood the responsibilities that come with adulthood the blurred lines between good and evil etc haunting of the hill house originally adapted from a book almost everything in the series represents or means something whether it is how an object in the background is positioned or how a character sees the world around them with so many ways to analyze how the plot unfolds and hundreds of literary devices and symbols spread across it the matrix a popular take on the age old philosophical debates on mind vs brain consciousness ai reality simulation theory etc psycho pass based on a dystopian world that questions our beliefs on morality and justice by pushing them to the extremes the exorcist leaves it on the viewer to interpret whether the events that unfold were the result of something paranormal or a grossly misdiagnosed schoolgirl experiencing a bizarre psychological disorder and many others like these']

Name: 104_words_sound_letter_sounds
Representative_Docs: ['gt have you been using words as examples where the target sound is in the middle or at the end cause when i first start with the letter s we ll look at words like grass and dress too and with airplane you have airplaine so there is an n sound just at the end of the word as for i i explain it more in the context of a sad mouse crying because she is dirty so their connection is with a sad mouse not with inky mouse i m in kindergarten so we are really stressing the beginning sounds at this level usually when they tell me airplane i ll acknowledge there is an n sound but we need something that starts with n inky is a little bit tricky because of the vowel sound and it was another teacher who taught them inky so i don t know exactly what she taught gt but yeah some of the references are a bit british xd that s my main issue the british references don t make sense to kids in rural saskatchewan although there is probably no mass produced phonics program that is perfect for that context gt though some are good for my esl students since i can show them a picture of a flamenco dancer and point to the country of spain on the map for instance they forget what the heck castanets are but they remember what the dance is like and the clacking sound the flameco dance makes i m glad this works for you i ve tried similar with my kids and it just doesn t click unless they ve got real experience with it gt edit do you ask the kids to do sound segmenting with their fingers that might help them remember like with airplane use one finger for each sound air p l ai n though dang that s a difficult word to segment most of mine are not ready to segment yet some of them are still working on recognizing the letters', 'first grade teacher here i ve been teaching st for seven years and am currently getting my master s in reading science i hope some of this info helps based on what you have observed amp shared in your post if he was my student i would not be concerned at all letter reversals in writing are developmentally appropriate still sounds like he can decode sound out words with phonetic patterns that are developmentally appropriate for this time of year which is great i m impressed that he can sound out multisyllabic words like himself every district school curriculum is different so take everything in the rest of my post with a grain of salt the curriculum we have doesn t even teach the vowel consonant e pattern until around february march words such as bake made note etc we also don t introduce vowel teams ea ee oi oy ay ai etc until march april that march end of year window is when we introduce multisyllabic words too at this time of the year i would expect my students to be able to read a text that includes the phonetic patterns that we ve learned we can t expect kids to read patterns they haven t been taught based on our curriculum a first grader at this point in the year should be able to read a book with sight words that they have learned consonant vowel consonant cvc words words with digraphs sh ch th wh ck if these have been taught consonant blend words ccvc as for leveled books i could rant for hours lol simply put most leveled books are full of sight words and words that contain phonetic patterns that students haven t been taught yet a big warning i always give my families is that most of the leveled books out there on the market think i can read or step into reading are all leveled by the publishers each publisher uses a different system to level their texts and most of them do a poor job anyways the focus should be on your child s decoding skills the ability to sound out the sounds in a word amp blend them together to read words can he fluently decode the phonetic patterns that he has been taught if yes then he is good to go if he has learned more advanced phonetic patterns like the silent e pattern already and is having a hard time with it try some practice with decoding words in isolation to develop the fluency then move to the sentence level sorry for the long reply i m so passionate about reading instruction that i just couldn t help myself', 'i will give you some tips but first i must say do not overwhelm a toddler with rigorous academic study be consistent but stick to only to minutes of planned phonics a day kids are going to learn with time and patients a toddler will become frustrated if forced to sit and practice letters for more than minutes you don t want them to run away or be upset with you when it is time to learn this has to be done with lots of praise and encouragement learning to read is difficult consistency is the best way to teach a child to read find a routine and stick to it here is the routine we used to teach our preschoolers to read do not go past step until your child has mastered letter sounds after this stick to the same step and reviewing previous steps before moving on daily abc practice saying the letter name and sounds abc songs bingo song interactive game board for letter sound recognition read a book a day when they begin to understand blending of cvc word then have them read beginner books like bob books once they have a grasp on identifying letters and their sounds begin to teach cvc words c a t is a cvc word it is a consonant vowel short sound consonant these letter words are easy to read and are a great confidence booster for new readers introduce sight words sight words are high frequency words that appear often in the english language you can always find a list of sight words with a quick internet search i suggest learning these words first i the a it an of on see look at write down short sentences for your child to read these sentences should only include cvc words and the sight words learned introduce cvce words these words have a consonant vowel consonant and silent letter e the letter e makes the vowel in the middle say its name long sound example h u g turns to h u g e and m a d turns to m a d e introduce more sight words introduce starting clusters and ending clusters example sh ch tr sp introduce vowel combinations example ai in maid ee in seed oo in book oi in boil continue to move up in the level of books they read encourage and praise the child as they progress focus on what good they have done learning to read is difficult but it does not mean a parent should not attempt to teach a child how']

Name: 107_cyber security_security_cyber_advanced level
Representative_Docs: ['colleges especially technical ones will have courses specifically for cyber security cyber security doesnt involve a tonne of programming at all it more involves configuring of hardware with tools port management setting up hardware the right way installing monitoring tools etc at least not in comparison to a software dev you still will do some programming to help with setting stuff up with things like ansible or configuring perhaps a kubernetes cluster at larger scale lots of writing scripts to automate your job and make it easier ai engineer is going to be computer sciences it involves a lot of math especially linear algebra and calculus so make sure you start getting a head start on calculus for sure and start learning the basics of linear algebra be familiar with what a matrix and vector is at high school level ai engineering is going to be mostly math and explaining to a lot of people no you cant do that with ai ai is better at doing this no thats not what that means you could do that but it would take a lot of work and money cyber security is going to heavily involve setting up servers installing hardware on racks installing software on those servers configuring switches and routers being really good at terminating ethernet cables a small bit of electronic engineering and a lot of pissing and swearing at linux trying to make it just do the damn thing you want it to do', 'if you want to get into the technical side of cyber security i e pentest appsec infra do a computer science degree preferably a school with either an infosec cs major or a school that has a few information security courses i don t trust cyber security specific schools especially those with a high amount of non technical curricula that stuff is important don t get me wrong but the first priority is building up your tech skills that s what is needed in the industry and how you distinguish yourself if you want a non technical role they are there and plentiful but it s an uphill battle to get one breaking into the security industry is harder than getting an entry level programming job otoh people with both infosec and programming skills are very valuable especially if you take a software engineering job or network engineering devops etc these jobs can be had with a cs degree make sure to keep up your general it skills sharp during your degree set up your own servers at home play around with firewall rules on your router stay awake during your networking course that is very very important no matter how boring it is know your osi model very very well entry level certs ex certified ethical hacker network security can help oscp is a highly respected cert that i personally would aim for if i was starting out and wanted to do red team type work i recommend getting your first job first in whatever it field you get first because it most likely won t be security and then getting a company to pay for you to take them cyber security is a specialization of computer science going into a cyber security degree right away without going through the basics is a bit like signing up for a machine learning bootcamp think about it you should be creating web applications even just basic ones before you can talk about securing them you should understand how containers work and what they re useful for before you even think about trying to harden them the big professional service firms ey deloitte kpmg pwc are always hiring for cyber security roles even for people without the education for it but you have to be an over achiever to get these i e do a ton of extra curriculars and you have to network it s also not the easiest place to work but you will get your foot in the door', 'i m also a cyber security master student i do not know how your curriculum looks like but based on my curriculum you do not need to know programming on an advanced level in cyber security if you are not sure if you need it on advanced level or not don t just assume things clarify them then ask from professors cyber security itself has multiple paths one can take not all of the paths require programming and not all of the programming paths require programming on advanced level i will list the paths you can take as a cyber security student international relations and international jurisdiction you ll be specializing in cyber security law and how it is applied when for example russian hackers are attacking services in ukraine latest events in the world what steps to take how to clarify that it was another country etc so international relations and international jurisdiction no programming there forensics you will be reading and investigating logs reading sensitive information out from images and such digital fingerprinting investigating system time stamps and their validity retrieving data from hard disks usb sticks phones etc you will not need really programming knowledge or only a minimum amount of it to write some scripts but usually there are already tools for extracting data for reading logs etc so you will need to know only how to configure the software and how to work with it no programming secure software development you ll be writing software by following different cyber security standards making the software secure against different threats and attackers there you will need to learn programming cryptography you will not need programming but mathematics a lot of theoretical mathematics maybe programming only a bit applied cryptography you will need programming as you will be applying the cryptography and writing different crypto software https tls ssh different other protocols online voting public private key cryptography blockchains crypto currency etc system administration you will not need really programming only as much to configure different environments fix library dependencies log events monitor these events for certain threats set up alerting also you might be configuring routing of the internet traffic switches etc cyber security awareness trainer you will not need programming you will be training the security awareness in schools companies etc also implementing different standards and courses web application security owasp you will need programming only as much to understand how the applications are consuming information and processing it so programming on intermediate level not on advanced level you will be concentrating more on the places of data entry and exit and on the data itself reverse engineering you will need programming probably on advanced level not that sure as i did not take that path machine learning you will need programming on advanced level also you will need to learn the machine learning itself as well you will be processing different cyber security related data network traffic logs etc and analyzing it there can be some paths that are combining two or more of what i told and also some paths i forgot to mention but my point is that not all of the cyber security paths require programming from you and not all of the programming paths require it on advanced level only few require advanced programming you have to decide for yourself which path you will be taking and then based on that path you should concentrate on your courses']

Name: 108_udacity_week_course_data
Representative_Docs: ['i graduated with my cs degree from wgu in october the job hunt is tough without having experience with tools frameworks and the like most java developer jobs require spring framework experience in rest apis and the like data analyst jobs require experience in power bi or tableau and other stuff i stumbled upon udacity and they have nanodegrees for java developers and data analysts and such i m not expecting the certificate to be of value but is what you take away valuable the descriptions of the courses seem to cover what i need and they have you build projects using what you learn can anyone who used udacity attest to whether their programs helped them to learn and subsequently land a job because of what they learned i m leaning more towards data analytics and data science as my career route side note i m looking for structured learning not just following youtube tutorials', 'the course itself was weeks but it s not really weeks of learning for the data cohort we did the following data analysis weeks week sql relational data week nonrelational data week excel power query week power bi week data analysis mastery project data engineering weeks week python basics week advanced python week pandas and visualizations week machine learning basics in python week kafka which is largely python week applied kafka capstone project weeks and capstone project the main skills to put on your resume would be python excel and power bi but you can add sql as well it s just that sql is week so it s easy to forget but with some review you should be confident in your sql capabilities i can truly say that i learned a lot here and i m very proud of what i ve accomplished it s pretty amazing how much they were able to teach in the span of a few months i mean yeah you have to be there for years but it s not like you re doing training for years you get placed with another company and really work for them though you still have to report a little to genesis so yeah they do force you to be with them for years but it s years and the company you work for may buy out your contract honestly i haven t been concerned about it at all since i started working here you need absolutely no programming background it helps but many people in my cohort were entirely new to it just be ready for python though it goes quickly and it may be hard for people without experience', 'i have done udacity nanodegrees edx coursera datacamp codecademy and udemy courses over the years i can t speak for udacity nanodegrees of today but overall i would not say udacity is a scam though i would say that the value of udacity is very different from the others back when i did it hopefully still so the nanodegrees were developed in collaboration with the likes of google and facebook and it showed out of all of the platforms it was the most industry oriented i ended up completing data analyst machine learning engineer data engineer and full stack web developer before i get to the cons first the pros the curriculum were standard setting da was data wrangling exploratory data analysis and data visualisation ml was regression classification clustering dimensionality reduction deep learning and reinforcement learning de was sql database nosql database etl data warehouse elt data lake pyspark and orchestration using airflow there was not a de course anywhere else at the time and was a trend setter in grouping data warehouse data lake spark airflow as a stack udacity also seemed to be the only place where data cleaning in dand taught how to structure the notebook logically when data cleaning the projects these were a first as far as i can tell where you could resubmit as many times as possible with relatively fast feedback from human reviewers i also felt a sense of trust that others who have done the nanodegree would have had project experience not just theoretical knowledge the video lessons alone are not sufficient for you to complete these project in first try though they give you all of the necessary starting points they weren t just some code alongs or follow the recipe types you needed to wrestle with documentation and stackoverflow forums but they weren t completely throwing you into the deep end either there were more than enough skeleton codes in the project templates the reviews came within hours most of the time and provided areas for improvement with code snippets or links to relevant material outside of udacity i wish university degrees were like this the cons there were some gaps between the level presented in video lessons compared to the level required in projects i had no issues because i had coding experience other than r and python but i did think that complete newcomers to programming would struggle unfortunately the lecturer standards were not consistent some mumbled some boring and unenthused but some were great some videos were outdated though the reading material that was provided pointed them out price kept creeping up i would think of it as how many times i submit projects per month and the value of receiving feedback like if i were to hire a tutor to take a look at my university assignments so yeah not perfect and the price to value is questionable but not a scam project curation and review feedback system is second to none on a side note i see great value with datacamp edx and coursera each with unique values udemy is cheap sure i ve seen many it graduates use it it doesn t seem to improve their troubleshooting skills or their code quality']

Name: 114_adhd_caffeine_medication_accommodations
Representative_Docs: ['gt doesn t explicitly support the claim either that should suffice it brings up dopamine some but it is still the same shape as a neurotransmitter that helps us get tired caffeine likes to jam itself in there to block its action we don t have enough dopamine but we still get tired lol another pop sci but still helpful majority of people with adhd will tell you no our meds don t really make us sleepy not like that at least and caffeine doesn t either ops thing about being a zombie kid on adhd meds is probably about emotional blunting and they can t remember anyways as stated adhd is hard to live with enough without people acting like drinking coffee and having a tolerance or jut not getting the desired effects is like an adhd diagnosis', 'this sounds like your own stigma projected onto your employer sure some employers are ableist and shitty but good ones will see how accommodations enable you to do your work well if you re uncomfortable disclosing memory issues you may be more comfortable disclosing adhd as it s super common and in no way in conflict with being a knowledge worker adhd is often linked with higher intelligence so there s less stigma than with other mental health conditions your employer doesn t need to be made aware of the nature of your disability just the limitations and accommodations that help you to do your job better a good psychiatrist could diagnose and come up with a treatment plan for your adhd and help request accommodations if you decide to go that route like using transcription ai to record meetings a lot of the usual accommodations for adhd like getting written directions also help those with memory issues you most likely need to see a psychiatrist who has familiarity with traumatic brain injury talking to a doctor is the first step you can decide what to disclose and when once you have an official diagnosis and treatment plan you should not return to recording meetings without an accommodation in place however as that is likely to get you fired if you do it without your employer s knowledge and consent you should also not put on a persona to try to excuse errors lapses as a persona is not a protected class you do have multiple disabilities and if you are over you fall into two protected classes it s time to get smart about how you recognize and cope with your limitations and get help to do so for peace of mind you may want to consult an employment lawyer or askjan org before requesting accommodations askjan org is also a great resource for possible tools to help with the things you re struggling with good luck', 'tldr lmao i have adhd i recommend napping meds hydration and pretty colors do you have other timely responsibilities outside of sleep and school like a job kids someone you have to care for animals etc if not i would say study at whatever time your brain is most retentive for me i study the best at extreme hours like wake up at am and study before everyone wakes up or go to bed at am and study after studying all night also if you study in large segments try doing lots of reps like exercise i like to switch it up so i never know what my time slots are going to look like until i start for example i ll study for minutes stretch for do a hobby for repeat or if something comes up while studying then i ll improvise and say okay in minutes i will spend minutes doing this task i then set a timer on my phone or on my watch i also got a set of cheap kitchen timers and they work too but the sound stresses me out and i feel bad for being so loud lmao i ve also become the queen of naps i nap in the morning for about minutes i always pretty much nap after dinner sometimes up to an hour i have gotten to where if exhaustion hits me then i just drop and nap for about minutes always set an alarm i have sleep masks and ear plugs that help too there s been times i ve even napped in the grocery store parking lot because being out in public exhausted me i m also a chronic illness mystery so my nap importance might be that or adhd or both lol i ve prioritized napping chugging one glass of water at a time that way it s out of the way and i won t have to remember i ve switched to caffeine mints and pills and less coffee that way i can get in more water i sometimes take a caffeine pill or mint and nap then when i wake up i can focus more also if you are on adhd medication i d recommend to set timers for when it s time to take them also i take my pill with a fat of sorts like a slice of cheese or in very desperate times i ll take my pills with cream lmao the fat content does something and somehow makes the medication more effective but if you re not on medication i d recommend trying to get that going if you don t know where to go you could try your student health resources i have pcp s therapists and specialists for second opinions since i get free care at my university i also take gaba chewable supplements at night the standard dose is but i take since i will sleep for like hours at a time on gaba does something like increase dopamine levels in your brain which counteracts cortisol which makes me relax enough to sleep much better def research i m just spouting a gist my therapist told me you can t take it with some meds also one last tip is at the beginning of each term make spreadsheet of all assignments due in all classes and sort them into chronological order you can print it off or keep it up on your screen and mark it off as you go i also have a notepad app on my computer and at the beginning of every week i will write down my tasks assignments and other big things i need to do i hope some of those help though i would say to alter things that fit your brain chemistry the best for me i m a very visual and auditory learner so i have colorful gel pens for note taking i have a printer so i can print out tools or notes if i want i m cursed with a hearing impairment so i also learn best if i can read whatever is being said so i use live captioning apps like otter ai']

Name: 115_calculus_linear_algebra_linear algebra
Representative_Docs: ['if you want to learn machine learning you absolutely need calculus also take your stats course seriously modern ml is basically high dimensional applied statistics if your required stats course does not involve multi variate calculus you need to take a higher level version edit also linear algebra high dimensional math in practice means matrices and tensors', 'i think it depends on what you want to do with machine learning of people in the field apply existing machine learning algorithms to new data you don t need to understand a lot of the advanced math in order to do that statistics is super helpful you definitely need to know the basics of linear algebra matrices vectors but not the advanced stuff if you want to actually understand machine learning algorithms and do new research you ll not only need the math you ll realistically need a master s or a ph d', 'make a distinction here between being a ml practitioner engineer and a researcher data scientist if you primarily want to implement ml in a business context you won t need much calculus and you can get by with a rudimentary knowledge of probability theory you will benefit from a working knowledge of basic calc but i wouldn t say it s strictly necessary unless you want to dedicate your applications to deep learning remember that much like all software engineering there is a lot of value to be gained in industry from well applied basics like a good linear regression if you want to create new algorithms and contribute to the forefront of research you will need a good knowledge of probability calculus and linear algebra to get started i would suggest that you need to know probability up to and including bayes theorem calculus up to and including partial differentiation and linear algebra such that you are comfortable reasoning about matrix manipulation and arithmetic the calculus is increasingly important if you want to work on neural networks and the lin alg is increasingly important if you re working on neural networks or images i find that analytic geometry is also helpful although typically overlapping with linear algebra calculus is one of those subjects where i find students either get it or they don t for those that get it full disclosure that i was not one of them in undergrad a semester s worth of study or say weeks of work on khan academy will prepare you for a more applied role presuming you have no current knowledge of calculus but you have a good grasp on all of the prerequisites if you don t get it it s going to be longer to move into research you typically want a phd anyway and your advisors are best suited to advise you funnily enough in that case the phd requirement is lowering over time though so consider your options before you believe me blindly on that one']

Name: 122_questions_explaining_star_ask
Representative_Docs: ['is it for a specific group or like the high schoolers in general maybe try to remember how you started getting into ai and start from there what are some classic ai tropes what even is ai what makes ai machine learning for example different from real intelligence and then go more in depth from there depending on how far you want to take it but i dont assume that you d want to introduce them on data science or how to properly train an ai or do you maybe give them some examples where ai is used in everyday life i think that the most important part is connecting ai with their lifes and giving them some easily comprehensible examples if you re really into ai machine learning you could even think about making a little chatbot to answer some common questions although that is indeed quite some work about the questions dont be insecure you dont have to know everything but you can be sure that you ll know more than them in general just refresh you general knowledge and maybe search for often asked questions online hope i could give you some ideas', 'i m a science communicator it s what i do for a living here are my main tips start with explaining the big stuff before you go into details focus on the context what actual real life problems for real people is your research involved in solving many researchers tend to focus on their particular bit but the public is interested in the overall topic for example i work with a statistician who does insanely complex eh statistics stuff on data from a computer game that schoolkids play in order to learn how to read the project is on reading and writing difficulties i don t think he is particularly interested in reading and writing he is interested in the computer modelling and statistics stuff but he is an excellent communicator so he always starts by talking on how too many kids struggle with reading and that maybe there are certain patterns in their development that can be found by analysing their behaviour in this game then he talks a bit about these kids and how the game is fun to play he touches upon his own analysis in layman s terms i m looking for patterns artificial intelligence is really interesting etc without using a single academic term the audience is always fascinated on that note leave the academic language where it belongs in academic journals it doesn t matter if you are using imprecise terms and words when communicating to a general audience look for good metafors start with the conclusion academics tend to start telling story the way they write academic articles a long background bit which is simply not interesting again leave that for the academic readership what have you found what are the implications why is this important again don t be afraid to be general don t be afraid to simplify this is not a phd defence you can always provide more details if people ask important see yourself as a communicator of your discipline not only of your research it always strikes me that say a school teacher with a ba in a certain subject would often be so much better at explaining concepts than full professors i guess it s the curse of knowledge one tends to not know the extent of one s own expertise try to keep in mind that the audience will have high school knowledge about your field at best don t think you are dumbing down you are not start really basic and build upon that learn from the best watch ted talks from people in or near your field and pay attention to what they leave out and what they include and reach out and ask for help from your university s communication department if you have one or a local science journalist for example just let them know you are interested in being a source just be open about the fact that you find this hard but that you are willing to learn let them lead the way and see how it goes find good popular science editors and collaborate they will be ruthless and very helpful good luck', 'i think it would be useful to create an intro video explaining the kinds of questions you expect if you say lead software engineer there s a strong implication you will teach programming if that s not your intent you need to phrase it differently such as explaining technology and propose questions just as you did that you would prefer to answer the hard part is getting people to ask good follow up questions for example what is ai you might respond in some way and the other person might say thanks and have no followup so there s no dialog or you could have them send you questions and you answer without interaction if you plan to have interaction i d say do a rehearsal which you can record but not upload and see if there s any interaction like i know stuff but i can act dumb enough to pepper you with question after question most people aren t that good in asking questions they need to be curious enough and thoughtful enough to have spent time on the question i ll give you an unrelated one from a recent askscience post this guy was thinking that waves travel through a medium sound travels through air waves travel through water and so forth so he reasoned there must be something that light must be traveling through if it s a wave how can there be no medium now that s a pretty thoughtful question that came from a fair bit of thinking turns out this is a pretty old idea back around the s people imagined there was this thing called ether due to how fast light traveled ether had to be rigid not sure why but anyway and yet planets would be traveling through this ether without much if any resistance even two researches called michaelson and morley conducted an interference test hypothesizing if there was an ether how it would likely behave and found that there was no evidence of this so would people think how does instagram work or they never gave it half a thought they post pics it seems to work done same with amazon most people have no idea how all that technology works they order something it arrives they re good even apps people have smart phones they use apps why would they ask what one is do you see what i m getting at there s not the same curiosity of technology as there is of physics or phenomenon in space a recent post about space had a picture of some star which was some number of light years away some had asked why did we pick that star when there s a star light years away much closer and the answer was the star that was further away was physically much larger times whereas alpha centauri i think has a star about as big as the sun this was the same with that black hole image a few years back they picked one further away because it was that much larger than the one closer also you did post to r learnprogramming which is a subreddit where most posters are attempting to learn how to program your title says you are a software engineer is it not surprising that those that respond to you would ask programming questions and not interesting ones the questions are often like what programming language should i learn am i too dumb to program do i need math to program i can t even solve easy leetcode problems what s the best course for x can i get a job remote working how long before i am ready to interview is it ok if i learn or more languages at the same time which career path should i take web dev game dev ml ai these get asked a lot rather than specific programming issues i think these kinds of questions would bore you to death so doing a mock interview first to see how things go and saying in advance for this to work you need to ask me good questions and good followups so we ll practice and see how that goes i reserve the right not to redo the interview if the interaction isn t good you would also ask for the initial question in advance so you know to eliminate those questions you aren t interested in answering your intro video could also say i don t plan on answering questions like x i think if you follow some of these steps it will hopefully lead to a more predictable group of people and you are answering questions that you want to answer instead of what language should i learn first and which course should i take']

Name: 123_internship_offer_summer_company
Representative_Docs: ['i am a master s student studying applied math finishing my degree this december i had an internship in nyc over the summer i went into the internship thinking i would love the city and the work would be machine learning statistics applied to the finance industry dream career for me the latter was a focus in the interview process and reinforced by my manager early on in reality i was assigned work that was closer to software development they came back with a full time return offer and an offer to work part time during the semester i accepted both with the intention of looking for another job in a different city that is closer to what i want to do while working hrs a week for them now that i ve actually started the job i realize that it would take away time for me to search for jobs and network with alumni as well as have a negative impact on my performance in the classroom the money is good but i m regretting taking the job and think it may be better to shift into overdrive for finding a job for after graduation that i would be happier with should i quit burn any bridges with the people i met over the summer and continue my search for a job in a more favorable location', 'hi y all i m facing a bit of a conundrum with career options after graduation i am graduating this june with a bs in bioengineering and data science however all of my research during undergrad was mol bio cell work no programming although i do have a few python projects under my belt from coursework with this in mind i applied to a bunch of bioinformatics machine learning internships to see if i liked the other side at first i wasn t getting many offers so i also applied to a few research associate positions knowing i was very qualified i did actually end up getting a bio info position this summer yay at the same time i proceeded through interviews with research associate positions i was upfront that i wasn t looking to work a full time position till mid september when my internship ends i thought that would turn most companies away and just lead to good networking opportunities however there is a cell bio company that seems pretty intent on offering me a position willing to wait for me to start in mid sep but i don t think i can commit to a role so far in advance especially before i experience the bioinformatics industry and decide which track i want to pursue which was the whole point of the internship how should i navigate this my current ideas upon receiving an offer tell them i m not ready to commit so far in advance tell them i would be interested in talking again in august if similar positions were open no hard feelings should they rightfully choose to fill the role in the meantime deny going to the final round interview tell them i landed a bio informatics position that might lead to a return offer so i don t want to lead them on reiterate points from about revisiting in august fail to graduate so i can avoid entering the real
