Name: 124_learn_dev_dev ai_app dev
Representative_Docs: ['it really depends on what you want to learn if you want to learn web development there are a lot of things that you can learn learn react or angular and spring boot or the c equivalent then learn cloud native that way you learn about docker and kubernetes and really learn a lot of applicable skills if you like video games learn c and unity focus on learning how to decouple your code and game design patterns you ll learn a lot about good programming paradigms while doing something fun if you want to learn about machine learning do something over nlp you can create a nlp algorithm to handle searches on a website or something like that', 'there are plenty of free resources if you don t want a paid one pick a pet coding project that you re passionate about and work hard on it every day for example if you like pokemon build a pokedex app hands on learning is the best because your brain doesn t internalize it until you put it to use also as you re coding you ll realize where your knowledge is lacking when you get stuck and need help then just search for tutorials videos on those specific topics full stack is a good choice eventually but i d suggest spending week on front end then week on back end to decide which one you like better then i d stick with just until you master it before doing both if you re not easily overwhelmed you could still start with full stack if you prefer front end i d suggest html css javascript angular which actually uses a different flavor of javascript called typescript if you prefer back end i d suggest c sql any flavor they re all very similar postgresql is my personal favorite but there are more jobs using sql server you can get the developer edition for free amp x b as far as an ide try visual studio code it s free and extremely good and popular it works well with any technology stack amp x b p s i started a yt channel to teach coding by modding video games check it out and see if it s helpful to you', 'probably you will stick with things from them because all of what you mentioned web dev mobile app dev ai game dev they all are four separate fields on the beginner level you can work with all of them but when you want to become professional it will become hard for you to follow trends in all of them either way the go to advice for these things most likely is the following mobile app dev learn either java or kotlin java if you want to feel comfortable with a wide variety of existing tutorials kotlin if you want to prepare yourself for the future as kotlin is taking the place of java in mobile app development alternatively you can try react native for cross platform applications there are other alternatives as well but these are the main ones i would say ai learn python and machine learning probably also knowing data structures linear algebra and discrete mathematics is needed you will need different math game dev c and or c unreal engine is using c while unity is using c some level knowledge in math and physics is also needed as your things will be moving and thus following mathematical and physical rules if you want to make life like animations and effects but if you are fine that the apple in your game falls to the sky or falls horizontally then don t pay attention to math physics']

Name: 125_papers_reviewers_paper_mail
Representative_Docs: ['your experience is unfortunately not that uncommon in your subfield i am in ai but a different subfield see for example this arxiv paper the authors compared algorithms presented at top conferences only were reproducible and from those were beaten with symbolic approaches you can search your favourite engine for other similar papers this push to publish whatever results you have is a byproduct of experiencing an ai summer where everyone and their mother is trying to publish a ml paper conferences like aaai and neurips are getting thousands of submissions neurips got over in this is a crazy amount and even if a large number of them are crap they still hold up spcs and pcs reducing their time from checking and reproducing experiments presented thankfully at least neurips started talking about reproducibility and is moving towards making various previously optional checklists mandatory aaai and ijcai are also moving in that direction alongside a long overdue ethics declaration now coming back to your case in full honesty there is not much you can do you have raised your concerns you could push through it by involving the hod prefect dean integrity officer but nothing will happen the most likely response will be it run fine on my machine at that time if you do that you may even burn bridges unexpectedly my advice is tell your supervisor they can fight the good fight without your direct involvement and use this as an educational experience for your own work always include your code and datasets as supplementary files thankfully it is becoming mandatory and then publish them for others to use and reproduce your work', 'disclaimer i m not in academia gt perhaps the biggest offender i ve seen is the machine learning research community research in there is not reproducible you get published based on who you know and how well connected they are with big tech and most of the conferences and journals have big tech all over them so this is pretty much unavoidable can you provide examples of this that seems a rather bold and unfair statement aimed at an entire community of researchers i have been through a fair number of academic papers but as i am not in academia i am not sure how representative they are at any given time i ve never been through a machine learning paper specifically but i ve been through a fair amount of formal and informal compsci papers and almost every one i ve found has been reproducible even when they seem to have a lot of errors in spelling or whatever never be hasty to judge an author by something like that which some have is the machine learning subset of paper writers so different which papers are you talking about may i take a look regarding publish or perish it does sound like a bad way to do things those who learn and research and do science for a living should not be subjected to that kind of institutional stress many of the best papers are written by people who spend however long it takes to follow through on an idea and it would have been a loss to all of history if they had perished for lack of frequent publishing at the risk of being political i ve always felt that one of the virtues of something like ubi would be that scientists and artists and scientific artists and artistic scientists could afford to be less reliant on systems which don t empower them fully at the moment edited for clarity', 'i had my paper recently rejected by a journal but the rejection e mail didn t make sense to me my cover letter had expressed an important concern about a previous paper that the rejection e mail didn t address at all i began to suspect that the editor hadn t read the cover letter so i wrote to them just asking what s going on i just said that perhaps my paper hadn t been properly evaluated but in any case i would be very thankful if the editor could address the specific concern expressed in the cover letter i had even prepared a submission to a different journal but now they have sent me an e mail saying that they filed an appeal and that editors would re evaluate their decision over the next two weeks so now i m supposed to wait two weeks for a new decision does anyone know what this means is it just an automatic response to every e mail sent to editors about past rejections or does this mean that they have finally read the cover letter and found our concern at least worthy of a more thorough evaluation i should say that my e mail was not specifically about filing an appeal i m getting very frustrated with this journal it seems like every communication with them consists of canned responses that make zero references to the issues we raised it s just like talking to a chat bot actually i m starting to think that the editorial board is just an artificial neural network that runs on the submitted manuscripts to predict the number of citations and can only reply with preprogrammed responses i m just kidding but maybe it s true']

Name: 126_cons_pros_web dev_enjoy
Representative_Docs: ['hi apologies in advance for the long post but any advice here is appreciated i am currently trying to decide between a data science and a web dev boot camp and would like to know if my perception of each is accurate background i have held a few different roles in supply chain buying that have been on the analytical side what i enjoy most is building tools to automate processes provide answers and generally help make people s lives easier this has never been the core of my job i just enjoy being the go to person for this type of thing that being said it has generally been limited to excel recently i have started using power query power pivot and vba and really enjoy building this automated behavior out current situation i decided that i would like to go in a more technical direction with the ability to build tools products be the core of my job my natural choice was data science given my background but i have some reservations about just how technical this side can get ml ai etc even though i am comfortable with math to aid in this decision i created this pros cons list and would love your feedback on the accuracy and if you have anything to add ds pros i have existing data analysis experience easier to land a job i tend to have a more logical data driven mind although i have never really tested other avenues ds cons not excited about the prospect of employing stats ml or ai it may just be intimidation without knowing a ton about it not very passionate about business performance and being focused solely on financial performance i feel like many of these roles focus on efficiency profitability of business roles may be less clearly defined as it is a newer field competing against people with cs math stats masters or even phds web dev pros like the idea of building things and working toward a tangible product enjoy working with multiple stakeholders in the company building something people find value in enjoy getting feedback and improving upon more freedom for freelance opportunities remote work right now i have the freedom in my life to make a bigger career pivot which may not exist forever web dev cons i don t Name: 128_cnn_tensorflow_neural_deep learning

basics and work their way up if anyone has any type of direction they can point me in whether it be advice a tutorial or anything else please let me know thank you and if anyone is wondering because i Name: 138_node_trees_oo_class
Representative_Docs: ['here s an example of a question i was asked during an internship interview and what chatgpt said question in terms of object oriented programming explain to me the difference between an interface and an abstract class as if i didn t really understand programming chatgpt response in object oriented programming an interface defines a set of methods that a class must implement while an abstract class is a class that contains one or more abstract methods an abstract method is a method that is declared but does not have an implementation in other words an interface defines the behavior that a class must have while an abstract class provides a partial implementation of that behavior a class can implement multiple interfaces but it can only inherit from a single abstract class here s an example to help clarify the difference suppose you are creating a program that models different types of vehicles such as cars trucks and motorcycles in this program you could create an interface called drivable that defines methods for starting stopping and steering the vehicle then any class that represents a type of vehicle such as car or truck would need to implement the drivable interface by providing an implementation for each of the methods defined in the interface on the other hand you could create an abstract class called vehicle that contains common properties and methods that all vehicles have such as a make and model property and a method for honking the horn the car truck and motorcycle classes could then inherit from the vehicle class which would give them access to the shared properties and methods however since the vehicle class contains abstract methods the child classes would still need to provide their own implementations for those methods in summary an interface defines a contract for a class to implement while an abstract class provides a partial implementation that can be shared among subclasses perfect grammar and a perfect explanation absolutely wild', 'gt in concept i completely understand what they are and how they operate on hardware as well as what they re good for especially in relation to ai but ask me to write fact when they re teaching beginners which means all the examples are badly fake which means the conclusions you reach are suspect and it s no surprise that you end up in an ontological crisis about crushable cans vs can crushers or generic crushers that can also crush boxes while being confused about what computers do computers know things not quite in the same way that people know things but it s a much less bad analogy more formally they store numbers and then we assume those numbers mean something we care about many high level languages have some way to organize numbers and to remind ourselves what those numbers mean for example you probably don t want to add a number that represents color to one that represents weight even by accident so your programing tools should discourage that this organizational system is often called a type system or sometimes a schema if the language doesn t help you then you have to apply some kind of conventions and discipline on your own this is why javascript and python have design patterns that aren t needed in java or ada in oop a class can function as a data type but it doesn t have to function as a data type and that s one of the ways them to each other it can easily revert its changes or explore different scenarios in different threads a this kind of thing is called immutable data or pure programming and you can use it in oo or functional programming it s just good clean fun with very few drawbacks for one it can put more stress on the garbage collector so it s sometimes bad for performance the second kind of method would be an example of a mutable object the most characteristic feature of oo is that it allows you to explain things to a computer in terms of mutable or stateful objects an object is a collection of functions which are not idempotent because they remember previous calls to the object and can do different things depending on what they remember stateful objects are analogous to a gas pump or an atm machine you have to poke them in the correct order or they won t do what you want them to this means they actually are merely extra annoying baggage if a behavior can be wrapped up into a single function call but when behavior is hard or impossible to describe that way when the most natural way to think about behavior is a series of events function calls what are your choices oo can be a pretty good choice functional programming has an excessively clever trick called a state monad that magically turns all those function calls into an idempotent function but this will turn your head inside out or you just kind of wing it i most recently used oo in an video card game the cards that are in your hand are the result of a series of events drawing cards playing cards since i wanted to describe those events as function calls oo resulted in nice looking code but in the part of the code that makes the computer automatically play i didn t i copy over the information the ai needs that goes through some pure code to pick candidates at the very end there s a random choice so it s not quite idempotent but there s nothing particularly oo about that part of the code but would be much worse if the ai tries various things and then has to undo moves so that it ends up corrupting the game and maybe it ends up corrupting the game so i don t try to force that part of the code into a mutable object paradigm the resulting code is okay in an oo context but it s not specifically oo the nice thing is that because i was writing in kotlin most of these tools are readily available it can t do the most extreme tricks of oo like multiple inheritance and you usually shouldn t do extreme fp stuff like by the way this generic signature means it works with any monad but otherwise you can write what s reasonable and it ends up looking nice and being nice to read and debug']

Name: 139_datasets_data_project_collecting
Representative_Docs: ['hi there i ve been in a similar position to you building out a data science team from scratch with a master s first thing s first you don t need project managers without projects and you don t need team leads without teams unless you re immediately hiring entire teams i would suggest focusing from the ground up someone with a real background building and productionising data models and services they don t need to be a phd data scientist you re already kinda confident in the value of your models so you re looking more for devops and infrastructure that s the kind of stuff that will bite you in the arse if you skimp on it early never go into anything blind have clear goals and roles and expectations it can be tempting to take your early capital and run in various directions don t be cautiously ambitious and communicate with your stakeholders twice as much as you feel you need to', 'i m in this position currently op first year of a cv project about to start collecting my own data soon and have just done a project risk assessment covering what happens if i don t obtain enough i would say firstly have multiple sources of data if you re collecting your own images then also try to incorporate other existing datasets when possible email other research teams for their data even use datasets made for other purposes if you can if you re not planning to consider collecting your own images too and don t forget about augmenting the data you have or synthesising more if you re still left with little data then consider if you can incorporate that deep learning with small datasets is a hot topic at the moment and ultimately you can always change project it sucks to change your plans to something you re less passionate about but if you make obtaining data a priority early on and keep an eye out for other interesting tasks with abundand data you should have plenty of time to pivot', 'this is a common question as every beginner wants to know where to start their data science projects first of all don t think big and start from the basics clear all your basic knowledge of machine learning first take an example from real life problems for best practices make the process flow and target the milestones according to your pace next go for the online certification there are lots of websites and apps that you find on the internet today they provide courses and projects for data science and give credentials as well it will help you clear your vision even more certifications are also beneficial for your resume the next step is to read about the data science lifecycle today s businesses run on data companies require data to remain competitive and agile thus it is crucial that you as a data expert comprehend the business and the value your work brings each of the processes listed below is a separate project with something like a business grasp of the hook make an individual portfolio of your top data science projects after reading the process flow you know about the different tasks of data sciences keep all the projects of your interest in separate lists read about them and know their basics in another step you need to work on visualization and storytelling understand your project well and use the appropriate tools to do that']

Name: 147_robotics_controls_mechanical_control
Representative_Docs: ['agree with everything u fieldline said you can still do software with your mechanical engineering background as long as you can do any programming effectively then most employers won t care now you are going to have a harder time competing for those pure software roles that is more tailored to cs degrees i would try to look for software engineering positions that have a system you can relate too i m getting a master s at the end of this year too with the specialization in controls robotics with ai i m able to understand the kinematics of the robot and create the program software needed for it a job example i found recently nvidia has a job posting for a robotics software engineer controls and they ll accept a ms in mechanical engineering etc', 'control systems engineering has some very interesting jobs some in robotics some in navigation satellites rockets planes etc some in ai some in economics etc for example asml has entire teams that design control algorithms for the steering of their tin generator this requires simulink stateflow for algorithm development and c c for controller implementation someone with a cs background would likely work on the embedded implementation of these algorithms however most theory heavy r amp d jobs require phds when i interviewed at asml they told me most of their controls engineers had physics phds additionally control systems engineering is often confused with controls engineering the former works on control algorithms the latter works on programming plcs it makes this type of job a little hard to look for i am also unsure about how many openings there are for this type of job though control theory is used everywhere it seems like a pretty small field maybe someone else can chime in about the career prospects', 'i did an internship in robotics and the lead engineer was a bsme with a mscs he knew how the robot operated kinematically but also knew how to write the software and middleware so yes it s possible i m currently in my masters for me amp ae but have been taking quite a few robotics and machine learning cs courses and get my matlab python linux ros fix through those so you may not need to do mscs to get cs experience i have phd classmates that are ee but doing reinforcement learning as their research interest i feel if you went straight cs from the beginning sure you would be able to code very well but can be on very abstract intangible systems like data mining or machine learning which some people have a hard time grasping since it s not always a physical tangible system ex if i need to create a convolution layer to filter the data then skip connection and up convolute to output result what s the kernel size needed to produce a x output some people me sometimes need an object tangible system that they can physical touch or see then write a program like a robot or control system ex if i want the robot to move from point a and stop at point b how do you program the motor to spin and the laser detector to detect when it gets to b it s viable to do a traditional engineer discipline then do a master in cs to know how to program for that discipline i have a professor that is bs in civil ms in cs amp structural and phd in civil he is able to understand how building structures can have fatigue and can program a reinforcement learning model that can predict when the building will have more stress fractures my cent feel free to ask me anything though']

Name: 148_search_google_stackoverflow_results
Representative_Docs: ['the big problem is that the entire system of searching has become gamified through these seo tricks everybody is trying to pull other than the obvious adding reddit to everything i don t know how such a thing can be solved since there s a fine line between the best and the worst in search i doubt anyone would say that google is the worst of the major search engines and i doubt there are any smaller ones able to do a lot of what google does but clearly being somewhere proximate the best doesn t prevent the best from being pretty fucking bad this tendency for google to serve up entire pages full of ads is the first thing to really get on my nerves simply put we trust reddit because at least the discussions tend to be real where when we search we re just getting a bunch of fake bullshit from fake people maybe even ai generated garbage depending on what is being searched and as good as reddit is for a lot of what we need it s not like this site s quality is going to indefinitely maintain its high some would argue it has already passed that point i would count myself among them reddit was mostly reliable as a big free for all but it seems increasingly that there is a proscribed way of thinking and interacting and we ve no one to blame but ourselves since a lot of us not only endorsed that sort of change but continue to promote such something similar happened to facebook where they started enforcing a broad conformity that was perfectly fine for the majority of users whose banalities know no bounds being deeper than the mariana trench but it was the death knell for creatives on facebook who fled to other platforms the simple fact is that the desire for money will always outpace any belief in values or any amount of theoretical freedom what happened to tumblr and facebook is happening here now and yet people cheer it on as if they ve never seen it before but i digress the overall point here is that the internet is cultivating its own distrust google is only a middleman', 'this has happened a couple of times so i thought i d share i googled a question for hours i searched in stackoverflow for the answer i asked the question on reddit finally i gave up and started typing my long question into stackoverflow as i typed i noticed the similar questions box was updating i looked up there and didn t see any questions asking the same thing but when i finished typing out my long question with code samples i noticed the similar questions box updated one more time and now the suggestions were awesome there were a couple of copies of my question with excellent answers i clicked them and found my answer without having to post it seems like stackoverflow has an excellent search machine learning engine that uses your entire post as search text to recommend similar questions because of this sometimes just typing your question into stackoverflow as if you were posting will yield better search results and get you the answer you need', 'i am not sure if its just me but i have noticed that google as a search engine is becoming dead day by day now a days when i have issue i just search on stackoverflow website search through github issues read documentation or straight up just read the source code i feel like google has greatly reduced search results from stackoverflow and github issues and most search results that appear are websites that are not helpful at all also in past few years google has put limit on search results you cannot go more than pages into a search query it says it has more than a million results matching this query but shows nothing more than a hundred as a programmer i need anything any forum thats discussing the issue or has matched with search query google putting limit on how much it can show me and also doing so called intelligent search which hurts results even more because i want result that match query not what an ai thinks i might want based on my location cookies interpretation of query']
